verses.hindiTranslation: गीदड़ों के समूह कटकटाते, खाते, चिल्लाते, अघाते, डपटते। करोड़ों धड़ और सिर —

verses.original:
बोल्लहिं जो जय जय मुंड रुंड प्रचंड सिर बिनु धावहीं।
खप्परिन्ह खग्ग अलुज्झि जुज्झहिं सुभट भटन्ह ढहावहीं।।
बानर निसाचर निकर मर्दहिं राम बल दर्पित भए।
संग्राम अंगन सुभट सोवहिं राम सर निकरन्हि हए।।
verses.transliteration: Bollahi jo jaya jaya muṇḍa ruṇḍa pracaṇḍa sira binu dhāvahīṁ. Khapparinha khagg alujjhi jujjhahi subhaṭa bhaṭanha ḍhahāvahīṁ. Bānara nisācara nikara mardahi rāma bala darpita bhae. Saṅgrāma aṅgana subhaṭa sovahi rāma sara nikaranha hae.
verses.translation: Heads cry 'Victory! Victory!' as mighty headless trunks dash about. With skull-bowls and swords they grapple and fight; brave warriors topple other warriors. The monkeys crush the demon hosts, emboldened by Rama's strength. In the arena of war, warriors lie slain, struck by Rama's showers of arrows.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो 'जय-जय' बोलते — सिर बिना प्रचण्ड धड़ दौड़ते। खप्पर (ढाल) और खड्ग (तलवार) से लड़ते।

verses.original:
रावन हृदयँ बिचारा भा निसिचर संघार।
मैं अकेल कपि भालु बहु माया करौं अपार।।88।।
verses.transliteration: Rāvana hṛdayam̐ bicārā bhā nisicara saṅghāra. Maiṁ akela kapi bhālu bahu māyā karauṁ apāra.
verses.translation: Ravana thought in his heart, 'My demon army is destroyed. I am alone against many monkeys and bears. Let me create infinite illusions.'
verses.hindiTranslation: रावण ने हृदय में विचारा — राक्षसों का संहार हो गया। मैं अकेला — वानर-भालू बहुत। अपार माया करूँ।

verses.original:
देवन्ह प्रभुहि पयादें देखा। उपजा उर अति छोभ बिसेषा।।
सुरपति निज रथ तुरत पठावा। हरष सहित मातलि लै आवा।।
verses.transliteration: Devanha prabhuhi payādēṁ dekhā, upajā ura ati chobha biseṣā. Surapati nija ratha turata paṭhāvā, haraṣa sahita mātalī lai āvā.
verses.translation: The gods saw the Lord fighting on foot and were greatly distressed. Indra immediately sent his own chariot; Matali brought it joyfully.
verses.hindiTranslation: देवताओं ने प्रभु को पैदल देखा। हृदय में अत्यन्त क्षोभ उत्पन्न हुआ। इन्द्र ने अपना रथ तुरन्त भेजा।

verses.original:
तेज पुंज रथ दिब्य अनूपा। हरषि चढ़े कोसलपुर भूपा।।
चंचल तुरग मनोहर चारी। अजर अमर मन सम गतिकारी।।
verses.transliteration: Teja puñja ratha dibya anūpā, haraṣi caḍhe kosalapura bhūpā. Cañcala turaga manohara cārī, ajara amara mana sama gatikārī.
verses.translation: The chariot, a mass of radiance, was divine and incomparable. The king of Kosala mounted it joyfully. Its four swift horses were captivating — ageless, immortal, swift as the mind.
verses.hindiTranslation: तेजपुंज, दिव्य, अनुपम रथ। हर्षित होकर कोसलपुर के राजा (श्रीराम) चढ़े। चार चंचल, मनोहर घोड़े — अजर (बूढ़े न होने वाले)।

verses.original:
रथारूढ़ रघुनाथहि देखी। धाए कपि बलु पाइ बिसेषी।।
सही न जाइ कपिन्ह कै मारी। तब रावन माया बिस्तारी।।
verses.transliteration: Rathārūḍha raghunāthahi dekhī, dhāe kapi balu pāi bisēṣī. Sahī na jāi kapinha kai mārī, taba rāvana māyā bistārī.
verses.translation: Seeing Raghunath mounted on the chariot, the monkeys charged with renewed strength. Unable to endure the monkeys' assault, Ravana then spread his illusion.
verses.hindiTranslation: रथारूढ़ श्रीरघुनाथ को देखकर वानर विशेष बल पाकर दौड़े। वानरों की मार सही नहीं जाती।

verses.original:
सो माया रघुबीरहि बाँची। लछिमन कपिन्ह सो मानी साँची।।
देखी कपिन्ह निसाचर अनी। अनुज सहित बहु कोसलधनी।।
verses.transliteration: So māyā raghubīrahi bām̐cī, lachimana kapinha so mānī sām̐cī. Dekhī kapinha nisācara anī, anuja sahita bahu kosaladhanī.
verses.translation: That illusion had no effect on Raghubir, but Lakshmana and the monkeys believed it real. The monkeys saw countless demon armies, and many Ramas and Lakshmanas with them.
verses.hindiTranslation: वह माया श्रीरघुवीर ने पहचान ली। लक्ष्मण और वानरों ने सच मानी। वानरों ने राक्षसों की सेना देखी।

verses.original:
बहु राम लछिमन देखि मर्कट भालु मन अति अपडरे।
जनु चित्र लिखित समेत लछिमन जहँ सो तहँ चितवहिं खरे।।
निज सेन चकित बिलोकि हँसि सर चाप सजि कोसल धनी।
माया हरी हरि निमिष महुँ हरषी सकल मर्कट अनी।।
verses.transliteration: Bahu rāma lachimana dekhi markaṭa bhālu mana ati apaḍare. Janu citra likhita sameta lachimana jaham̐ so taham̐ citavahi khare. Nija sena cakita biloki ham̐si sara cāpa saji kosala dhanī. Māyā harī hari nimiṣa mahum̐ haraṣī sakala markaṭa anī.
verses.translation: Seeing many Ramas and Lakshmanas, the monkeys and bears were deeply frightened. Like painted pictures, they stood staring wherever they saw Rama with Lakshmana. Seeing his own army bewildered, the Lord of Kosala smiled, readied his bow and arrows, and in an instant destroyed the illusion. The entire monkey army rejoiced.
verses.hindiTranslation: बहुत राम-लक्ष्मण देखकर वानर-भालू मन में अत्यन्त डर गए। मानो चित्र में लिखे (जड़) — लक्ष्मण सहित जहाँ-तहाँ खड़े।

verses.original:
बहुरि राम सब तन चितइ बोले बचन गँभीर।
द्वंदजुद्ध देखहु सकल श्रमित भए अति बीर।।89।।
verses.transliteration: Bahuri rāma saba tana citai bole bacana gam̐bhīra. Dvandajuddha dekhahu sakala śramita bhae ati bīra.
verses.translation: Then Rama looked upon his entire army and spoke in a deep voice: 'Watch the single combat now. All you warriors are weary — rest.'
verses.hindiTranslation: फिर श्रीराम ने सबके शरीर को देखकर गम्भीर वचन बोले — (श्रीराम ने कहा) द्वन्द्वयुद्ध देखो सब — वीर अत्यन्त श्रमित हुए।

verses.original:
अस कहि रथ रघुनाथ चलावा। बिप्र चरन पंकज सिरु नावा।।
तब लंकेस क्रोध उर छावा। गर्जत तर्जत सन्मुख धावा।।
verses.transliteration: Asa kahi ratha raghunātha calāvā, bipra carana paṅkaja siru nāvā. Taba laṅkesa krodha ura chāvā, garjata tarjata sanmukha dhāvā.
verses.translation: So saying, Raghunath drove his chariot forward, bowing his head to the feet of the brahmins. Then Lanka's lord was seized with fury; roaring and threatening, he charged forth.
verses.hindiTranslation: ऐसा कहकर श्रीरघुनाथ ने रथ चलाया। ब्राह्मण-चरण-कमलों पर सिर नवाया। तब लंकेश के हृदय में क्रोध छाया।

verses.original:
जीतेहु जे भट संजुग माहीं। सुनु तापस मैं तिन्ह सम नाहीं।।
रावन नाम जगत जस जाना। लोकप जाकें बंदीखाना।।
verses.transliteration: Jītehu je bhaṭa sañjuga māhīṁ, sunu tāpasa maiṁ tinha sama nāhīṁ. Rāvana nāma jagata jasa jānā, lokapā jākēṁ bandīkhānā.
verses.translation: 'Those warriors you defeated in battle — listen, ascetic, I am not like them. My name is Ravana, famous throughout the world; the guardians of the world are in my prison.'
verses.hindiTranslation: (रावण ने कहा) जिन योद्धाओं को मैंने युद्ध में जीता — सुन तपस्वी! मैं उनके समान नहीं। रावण नाम — जगत-प्रसिद्ध यश।

verses.original:
खर दूषन बिराध तुम्ह मारा। बधेहु ब्याध इव बालि बिचारा।।
निसिचर निकर सुभट संघारेहु। कुंभकरन घननादहि मारेहु।।
verses.transliteration: Khara dūṣana birādha tumha mārā, badhehu byādha iva bāli bicārā. Nisicara nikara subhaṭa saṅghārehu, kumbhakarana ghananādahi mārehu.
verses.translation: 'You killed Khara, Dushana, and Viradha. You slew poor Vali like a hunter. You destroyed demon hosts and killed Kumbhakarna and Meghnad.'
verses.hindiTranslation: (रावण ने कहा) खर-दूषण-विराध तुमने मारे। व्याध (शिकारी) की भाँति बेचारे बालि को मारा। राक्षस-समूह — श्रेष्ठ योद्धाओं का संहार।

verses.original:
आजु बयरु सबु लेउँ निबाही। जौं रन भूप भाजि नहिं जाहीं।।
आजु करउँ खलु काल हवाले। परेहु कठिन रावन के पाले।।
verses.transliteration: Āju bayaru sabu leum̐ nibāhī, jauṁ rana bhūpa bhāji nahiṁ jāhīṁ. Āju karauṁ khalu kāla havāle, parehu kaṭhina rāvana ke pāle.
verses.translation: 'Today I shall settle all my enmity — if you do not flee the battle, O king! Today I shall consign you to Death. You have fallen into the clutches of Ravana.'
verses.hindiTranslation: (रावण ने कहा) आज सारा बैर निबटा लूँगा — यदि रणभूमि से राजा भाग न जाएँ। आज इस दुष्ट (रावण स्वयं) को काल बना दूँगा।

verses.original:
सुनि दुर्बचन कालबस जाना। बिहँसि बचन कह कृपानिधाना।।
सत्य सत्य सब तव प्रभुताई। जल्पसि जनि देखाउ मनुसाई।।
verses.transliteration: Suni durbacana kālabasa jānā, biham̐si bacana kaha kṛpānidhānā. Satya satya saba tava prabhutāī, jalpasi jani dekhāu manusāī.
verses.translation: Hearing his harsh words and knowing him to be in Death's grip, the merciful Lord smiled and said: 'True, true, all your boasts. But do not merely talk — show your manhood now.'
verses.hindiTranslation: दुर्वचन सुनकर (श्रीराम ने) कालवश जाना। हँसकर कृपानिधान ने वचन कहे — (श्रीराम ने कहा) सत्य-सत्य, सब तेरी प्रभुता।

verses.original:
जनि जल्पना करि सुजसु नासहि नीति सुनहि करहि छमा।
संसार महँ पूरुष त्रिबिध पाटल रसाल पनस समा।।
एक सुमनप्रद एक सुमन फल एक फलइ केवल लागहीं।
एक कहहिं कहहिं करहिं अपर एक करहिं कहत न बागहीं।।
verses.transliteration: Jani jalpanā kari sujasu nāsahi nīti sunahi karahi chamā. Sansāra maham̐ pūruṣa tribidha pāṭala rasāla panasa samā. Eka sumanaprada eka sumana phala eka phalai kevala lāgahīṁ. Eka kahahi kahahi karahi apara eka karahi kahata na bāgahīṁ.
verses.translation: 'Do not waste words and ruin your fair fame. Listen to wisdom and show forbearance. In the world there are three kinds of men, like the coral tree, the mango, and the jackfruit. One gives flowers, one gives both flowers and fruit, and one gives only fruit. Some speak and then act, others act and never boast.'
verses.hindiTranslation: (श्रीराम ने कहा) व्यर्थ बकवास करके सुयश नष्ट मत करो। नीति सुनो, क्षमा करो। संसार में तीन प्रकार के पुरुष —

verses.original:
राम बचन सुनि बिहँसा मोहि सिखावत ग्यान।
बयरु करत नहिं तब डरे अब लागे प्रिय प्रान।।90।।
verses.transliteration: Rāma bacana suni biham̐sā mohi sikhāvata gyāna. Bayaru karata nahiṁ taba ḍare aba lāge priya prāna.
verses.translation: Hearing Rama's words, Ravana laughed: 'You lecture me on wisdom! When you picked this quarrel, you had no fear; now your life has become precious?'
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीराम के वचन सुनकर (रावण) हँसा — (रावण ने कहा) मुझे ज्ञान सिखाता है! वैर करते समय तो नहीं डरे — अब प्रिय वचन (सिखाने) लगे!

verses.original:
कहि दुर्बचन क्रुद्ध दसकंधर। कुलिस समान लाग छाँड़ै सर।।
नानाकार सिलीमुख धाए। दिसि अरु बिदिस गगन महि छाए।।
verses.transliteration: Kahi durbacana kruddha dasakandhara, kulisa samāna lāga chām̐ḍai sara. Nānākāra silīmukha dhāe, disi aru bidisi gagana mahi chāe.
verses.translation: Uttering harsh words, the wrathful ten-headed one began releasing arrows as hard as thunderbolts. Arrows of various kinds flew forth, covering every direction, the sky, and earth.
verses.hindiTranslation: दुर्वचन कहकर क्रुद्ध दशकंधर — वज्र-समान बाण छोड़ने लगा। अनेक प्रकार के बाण दौड़े।

verses.original:
पावक सर छाँड़ेउ रघुबीरा। छन महुँ जरे निसाचर तीरा।।
छाड़िसि तीब्र सक्ति खिसिआई। बान संग प्रभु फेरि चलाई।।
verses.transliteration: Pāvaka sara chām̐ḍeu raghubīrā, chana mahum̐ jare nisācara tīrā. Chāḍisi tībra sakti khisiāī, bāna saṅga prabhu pheri calāī.
verses.translation: Raghubir released a fire-arrow, and in an instant, Ravana's arrows were burned. Ravana hurled a terrible spear in anger; the Lord deflected it with an arrow.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीरघुवीर ने अग्नि-बाण छोड़ा — क्षण में राक्षस के तीर जल गए। खिसियाकर तीव्र शक्ति छोड़ी।

verses.original:
कोटिक चक्र त्रिसूल पबारै। बिनु प्रयास प्रभु काटि निवारै।।
निफल होहिं रावन सर कैसें। खल के सकल मनोरथ जैसें।।
verses.transliteration: Koṭika cakra trisūla pabārai, binu prayāsa prabhu kāṭi nivārai. Niphala hohi rāvana sara kaisēṁ, khala ke sakala manoratha jaisēṁ.
verses.translation: He hurled millions of discuses and tridents, but the Lord cut them aside effortlessly. Ravana's arrows became futile, just as all the desires of a wicked person come to naught.
verses.hindiTranslation: करोड़ चक्र-त्रिशूल फेंके। बिना प्रयास प्रभु ने काटकर रोक दिए। रावण के बाण निष्फल होते।

verses.original:
तब सत बान सारथी मारेसि। परेउ भूमि जय राम पुकारेसि।।
राम कृपा करि सूत उठावा। तब प्रभु परम क्रोध कहुँ पावा।।
verses.transliteration: Taba sata bāna sārathī māresi, pareu bhūmi jaya rāma pukāresi. Rāma kṛpā kari sūta uṭhāvā, taba prabhu parama krodha kahum̐ pāvā.
verses.translation: Then with a hundred arrows, Rama struck his charioteer, who fell to the ground crying 'Victory to Rama!' Rama's grace revived the charioteer. Then the Lord was seized with supreme anger.
verses.hindiTranslation: तब सौ बाणों से सारथी मारा। पृथ्वी पर गिरा — 'जय राम' पुकारा। श्रीराम ने कृपा करके सारथी उठाया।

verses.original:
भए क्रुद्ध जुद्ध बिरुद्ध रघुपति त्रोन सायक कसमसे।
कोदंड धुनि अति चंड सुनि मनुजाद सब मारुत ग्रसे।।
मँदोदरी उर कंप कंपति कमठ भू भूधर त्रसे।
चिक्करहिं दिग्गज दसन गहि महि देखि कौतुक सुर हँसे।।
verses.transliteration: Bhae kruddha juddha biruddha raghupati trona sāyaka kasamase. Kodaṇḍa dhuni ati caṇḍa suni manujāda saba māruta grase. Mam̐dodarī ura kampa kampati kamaṭha bhū bhūdhara trase. Cikkarahi diggaja dasana gahi mahi dekhi kautuka sura ham̐se.
verses.translation: The Lord grew furious in battle; the arrows in his quiver quivered with eagerness. Hearing the terrible twang of his bow, the demons were seized by panic. Mandodari's heart trembled; the tortoise, earth, and mountains quaked with fear. The elephants of the quarters trumpeted, gripping the earth with their teeth. Seeing this spectacle, the gods laughed.
verses.hindiTranslation: क्रुद्ध होकर युद्ध-विरुद्ध श्रीरघुपति — तूणीर और बाण कसमसाए। धनुष की प्रचण्ड ध्वनि सुनकर — मेघनाद-समान वीरों के मन डोले।

verses.original:
तानेउ चाप श्रवन लगि छाँड़े बिसिख कराल।
राम मारगन गन चले लहलहात जनु ब्याल।।91।।
verses.transliteration: Tāneu cāpa śravana lagi chām̐ḍe bisikha karāla. Rāma mārgana gana cale lahalahāta janu byāla.
verses.translation: He drew his bow to its full extent and released terrible arrows. Rama's arrows flew forth, writhing like serpents.
verses.hindiTranslation: कान तक धनुष खींचकर भयंकर बाण छोड़े। श्रीराम के बाणों के समूह चले — लहलहाते, मानो साँप।

verses.original:
चले बान सपच्छ जनु उरगा। प्रथमहिं हतेउ सारथी तुरगा।।
रथ बिभंजि हति केतु पताका। गर्जा अति अंतर बल थाका।।
verses.transliteration: Cale bāna sapaccha janu uragā, prathamahi hateu sārathī turagā. Ratha bibhan̄ji hati ketu patākā, garjā ati antara bala thākā.
verses.translation: The arrows flew like winged serpents. First they killed the charioteer and horses. They shattered the chariot and struck down the standard and flag. Ravana roared, but his inner strength was exhausted.
verses.hindiTranslation: पंखदार साँपों-से बाण चले। पहले सारथी और घोड़े मारे। रथ तोड़ा, ध्वजा-पताका मारी।

verses.original:
तुरत आन रथ चढ़ि खिसिआना। अस्त्र सस्त्र छाँड़ेसि बिधि नाना।।
बिफल होहिं सब उद्यम ताके। जिमि परद्रोह निरत मनसा के।।
verses.transliteration: Turata āna ratha caḍhi khisiānā, astra sastra chām̐ḍesi bidhi nānā. Biphala hohi saba udyama tāke, jimi paradroha nirata manasā ke.
verses.translation: Angrily he mounted another chariot and hurled weapons and missiles of every kind. But all his efforts proved futile, just like the schemes of one who delights in harming others.
verses.hindiTranslation: तुरन्त दूसरे रथ पर चढ़कर खिसियाया। अनेक प्रकार के अस्त्र-शस्त्र छोड़े। सब निष्फल हुए।

verses.original:
तब रावन दस सूल चलावा। बाजि चारि महि मारि गिरावा।।
तुरग उठाइ कोपि रघुनायक। खैंचि सरासन छाँड़े सायक।।
verses.transliteration: Taba rāvana dasa sūla calāvā, bāji cāri mahi māri girāvā. Turaga uṭhāi kopi raghunāyaka, khaiṁci sarāsana chām̐ḍe sāyaka.
verses.translation: Then Ravana hurled ten tridents, striking down the four horses. Raghunayak raised the horses and in anger drew his bow and released arrows.
verses.hindiTranslation: तब रावण ने दस शूल चलाए। चार घोड़ों को मारकर गिराया। घोड़े उठाकर क्रोधित श्रीरघुनायक ने — भयंकर बाण छोड़े।

verses.original:
रावन सिर सरोज बनचारी। चलि रघुबीर सिलीमुख धारी।।
दस दस बान भाल दस मारे। निसरि गए चले रुधिर पनारे।।
verses.transliteration: Rāvana sira saroja banacārī, calī raghubīra silīmukha dhārī. Dasa dasa bāna bhāla dasa māre, nisari gae cale rudhira panāre.
verses.translation: Rama's arrows wandered among Ravana's heads like bees among lotuses. He struck ten arrows into each of the ten foreheads; they emerged on the other side, streaming with blood.
verses.hindiTranslation: रावण के सिर-कमलों पर बाण-भ्रमर चले — श्रीरघुवीर के बाणों की धार। दस-दस बाण दसों मस्तकों पर मारे। निकले — फिर निकले।

verses.original:
स्त्रवत रुधिर धायउ बलवाना। प्रभु पुनि कृत धनु सर संधाना।।
तीस तीर रघुबीर पबारे। भुजन्हि समेत सीस महि पारे।।
verses.transliteration: Stravata rudhira dhāyau balavānā, prabhu puni kṛta dhanu sara sandhānā. Tīsa tīra raghubīra pabāre, bhujanha sameta sīsa mahi pāre.
verses.translation: Streaming with blood, the mighty one charged forth. The Lord readied bow and arrows again. Raghubir released thirty arrows and severed heads and arms, hurling them to the ground.
verses.hindiTranslation: रुधिर बहता — बलवान दौड़ा। प्रभु ने फिर धनुष-बाण संधान किया। तीस-तीस बाण श्रीरघुवीर ने फेंके।

verses.original:
काटतहीं पुनि भए नबीने। राम बहोरि भुजा सिर छीने।।
प्रभु बहु बार बाहु सिर हए। कटत झटिति पुनि नूतन भए।।
verses.transliteration: Kāṭatahīṁ puni bhae nabīne, rāma bahori bhujā sira chīne. Prabhu bahu bāra bāhu sira hae, kaṭata jhaṭiti puni nūtana bhae.
verses.translation: No sooner were they cut than new ones grew. Rama again severed arms and heads. The Lord cut arms and heads many times, but the instant they were cut, new ones appeared.
verses.hindiTranslation: काटते ही फिर नए उग आए। श्रीराम ने फिर भुजाएँ और सिर काटे। प्रभु ने बहुत बार बाहु-सिर काटे — (पर फिर उगते)।

verses.original:
पुनि पुनि प्रभु काटत भुज सीसा। अति कौतुकी कोसलाधीसा।।
रहे छाइ नभ सिर अरु बाहू। मानहुँ अमित केतु अरु राहू।।
verses.transliteration: Puni puni prabhu kāṭata bhuja sīsā, ati kautukī kosalādhīsā. Rahe chāi nabha sira aru bāhū, mānahum̐ amita ketu aru rāhū.
verses.translation: Again and again the Lord cut heads and arms — so playful is the king of Kosala! Severed heads and arms filled the sky, looking like countless Rahu and Ketu.
verses.hindiTranslation: बार-बार प्रभु काटते — भुजाएँ और सिर। अति कौतुकी कोसलाधीश। आकाश में सिर और बाहुएँ छा गईं।

verses.original:
जनु राहु केतु अनेक नभ पथ स्त्रवत सोनित धावहीं।
रघुबीर तीर प्रचंड लागहिं भूमि गिरन न पावहीं।।
एक एक सर सिर निकर छेदे नभ उड़त इमि सोहहीं।
जनु कोपि दिनकर कर निकर जहँ तहँ बिधुंतुद पोहहीं।।
verses.transliteration: Janu rāhu ketu aneka nabha patha stravata sonita dhāvahīṁ. Raghubīra tīra pracaṇḍa lāgahi bhūmi girana na pāvahīṁ. Eka eka sara sira nikara chedē nabha uḍata imi sohahīṁ. Janu kopi dinakara kara nikara jaham̐ taham̐ bidhuntuda pohahīṁ.
verses.translation: Like many Rahu and Ketu, the severed heads streamed with blood as they flew through the sky. Struck by Raghubir's fierce arrows, they did not even reach the ground. Each single arrow severed clusters of heads that flew about, looking as though the angry sun's rays were piercing through Rahu in every direction.
verses.hindiTranslation: मानो राहु-केतु अनेक — आकाश-मार्ग में रुधिर बहाते दौड़ रहे। श्रीरघुवीर के प्रचण्ड बाण लगते — भूमि पर गिरते।

verses.original:
जिमि जिमि प्रभु हर तासु सिर तिमि तिमि होहिं अपार।
सेवत बिषय बिबर्ध जिमि नित नित नूतन मार।।92।।
verses.transliteration: Jimi jimi prabhu hara tāsu sira timi timi hohi apāra. Sevata biṣaya bibardha jimi nita nita nūtana māra.
verses.translation: The more the Lord severed his heads, the more they multiplied — just as desire increases with every gratification, ever new temptations arising.
verses.hindiTranslation: जैसे-जैसे प्रभु सिर काटते — वैसे-वैसे अपार (नए) होते। जैसे विषय सेवन करने से (विषय-वासना) नित्य नई बढ़ती है।

verses.original:
दसमुख देखि सिरन्ह कै बाढ़ी। बिसरा मरन भई रिस गाढ़ी।।
गर्जेउ मूढ़ महा अभिमानी। धायउ दसहु सरासन तानी।।
verses.transliteration: Dasamukha dekhi siranha kai bāḍhī, bisarā marana bhaī risa gāḍhī. Garjeu mūḍha mahā abhimānī, dhāyau dasahu sarāsana tānī.
verses.translation: Seeing his heads multiply, Ravana forgot about death and was seized with fierce rage. The fool, supremely arrogant, roared and charged with all ten bows drawn.
verses.hindiTranslation: दशमुख ने सिरों की बढ़ती देखी — मरना भूल गया, गाढ़ा क्रोध हुआ। मूढ़ ने महा अभिमान से गर्जना की।

verses.original:
समर भूमि दसकंधर कोप्यो। बरषि बान रघुपति रथ तोप्यो।।
दंड एक रथ देखि न परेऊ। जनु निहार महुँ दिनकर दुरेऊ।।
verses.transliteration: Samara bhūmi dasakandhara kopyo, baraṣi bāna raghupati ratha topyo. Daṇḍa eka ratha dekhi na pareū, janu nihāra mahum̐ dinakara dureū.
verses.translation: On the battlefield, the enraged ten-headed one showered arrows and covered Raghupati's chariot. For a moment the chariot could not be seen, as though the sun had vanished in mist.
verses.hindiTranslation: समरभूमि में दशकंधर कुपित हुआ। बाण बरसाकर श्रीरघुपति का रथ ढँक दिया। एक दण्ड (24 मिनट) तक रथ दिखाई नहीं दिया।

verses.original:
हाहाकार सुरन्ह जब कीन्हा। तब प्रभु कोपि कारमुक लीन्हा।।
सर निवारि रिपु के सिर काटे। ते दिसि बिदिस गगन महि पाटे।।
verses.transliteration: Hāhākāra suranha jaba kīnhā, taba prabhu kopi kāramuka līnhā. Sara nivāri ripu ke sira kāṭe, te disi bidisi gagana mahi pāṭe.
verses.translation: When the gods raised a cry of despair, the Lord angrily took up his bow. He deflected the arrows and severed the enemy's heads; they scattered across all directions, sky, and earth.
verses.hindiTranslation: देवताओं ने जब हाहाकार किया — तब प्रभु ने क्रोधित होकर धनुष लिया। शत्रु के बाण रोककर — उसके बाण काटे।

verses.original:
काटे सिर नभ मारग धावहिं। जय जय धुनि करि भय उपजावहिं।।
कहँ लछिमन सुग्रीव कपीसा। कहँ रघुबीर कोसलाधीसा।।
verses.transliteration: Kāṭe sira nabha māraga dhāvahi, jaya jaya dhuni kari bhaya upajāvahi. Kaham̐ lachimana sugrīva kapīsā, kaham̐ raghubīra kosalādhīsā.
verses.translation: The severed heads flew through the sky, crying 'Victory! Victory!' and spreading terror. 'Where is Lakshmana? Where is Sugriva, the monkey king? Where is Raghubir, the lord of Kosala?'
verses.hindiTranslation: कटे सिर आकाश-मार्ग में दौड़ते। 'जय-जय' ध्वनि करके भय उत्पन्न करते। (सिर पुकारते) कहाँ लक्ष्मण? कहाँ सुग्रीव? कहाँ वानर?

verses.original:
कहँ रामु कहि सिर निकर धाए देखि मर्कट भजि चले।
संधानि धनु रघुबंसमनि हँसि सरन्हि सिर बेधे भले।।
सिर मालिका कर कालिका गहि बृंद बृंदन्हि बहु मिलीं।
करि रुधिर सरि मज्जनु मनहुँ संग्राम बट पूजन चलीं।।
verses.transliteration: Kaham̐ rāmu kahi sira nikara dhāe dekhi markaṭa bhaji cale. Sandhāni dhanu raghubansamani ham̐si saranha sira bedhē bhale. Sira mālikā kara kālikā gahi bṛnda bṛndanhi bahu milīṁ. Kari rudhira sari majjanu manahum̐ saṅgrāma baṭa pūjana calīṁ.
verses.translation: 'Where is Rama?' So crying, clusters of heads rushed forth. Seeing them, the monkeys fled. The jewel of the Raghu dynasty smiled, drew his bow, and neatly pierced the heads with arrows. Kalikas (forms of Kali) took garlands of heads in their hands and gathered in groups. Bathing in the river of blood, they seemed to set out to worship the banyan tree of battle.
verses.hindiTranslation: 'कहाँ राम?' कहते सिरों के समूह दौड़े — देखकर वानर भागे। रघुवंशमणि ने हँसकर धनुष संधानकर बाणों से —

verses.original:
पुनि दसकंठ क्रुद्ध होइ छाँड़ी सक्ति प्रचंड।
चली बिभीषन सन्मुख मनहुँ काल कर दंड।।93।।
verses.transliteration: Puni dasakaṇṭha kruddha hoi chām̐ḍī sakti pracaṇḍa. Calī bibhīṣana sanmukha manahum̐ kāla kara daṇḍa.
verses.translation: Then the furious ten-headed one hurled a fierce spear at Vibhishana — like the rod of Death itself.
verses.hindiTranslation: फिर दशकंठ क्रुद्ध होकर प्रचण्ड शक्ति छोड़ी। विभीषण के सामने चली — मानो काल का दण्ड।

verses.original:
आवत देखि सक्ति अति घोरा। प्रनतारति भंजन पन मोरा।।
तुरत बिभीषन पाछें मेला। सन्मुख राम सहेउ सोइ सेला।।
verses.transliteration: Āvata dekhi sakti ati ghorā, pranatārati bhañjana pana morā. Turata bibhīṣana pāchēṁ melā, sanmukha rāma saheu soi selā.
verses.translation: Seeing the terrible spear approaching, the Lord said, 'My vow is to protect those who seek refuge.' He instantly pushed Vibhishana behind him and himself received the blow.
verses.hindiTranslation: अत्यन्त घोर शक्ति आती देखकर — (श्रीराम ने सोचा) शरणागतों की पीड़ा मिटाना मेरा प्रण है। तुरन्त विभीषण को पीछे कर लिया।

verses.original:
लागि सक्ति मुरुछा कछु भई। प्रभु कृत खेल सुरन्ह बिकलई।।
देखि बिभीषन प्रभु श्रम पायो। गहि कर गदा क्रुद्ध होइ धायो।।
verses.transliteration: Lāgi sakti muruchā kachu bhaī, prabhu kṛta khela suranha bikalī. Dekhi bibhīṣana prabhu śrama pāyo, gahi kara gadā kruddha hoi dhāyo.
verses.translation: The spear struck, and the Lord swooned briefly — it was his divine play, but the gods were alarmed. Seeing the Lord fatigued, Vibhishana seized his mace and charged forth in fury.
verses.hindiTranslation: शक्ति लगी — कुछ मूर्छा हुई। प्रभु ने खेल किया — देवताओं को व्याकुलता हुई। विभीषण ने प्रभु की दशा देखकर —

verses.original:
रे कुभाग्य सठ मंद कुबुद्धे। तैं सुर नर मुनि नाग बिरुद्धे।।
सादर सिव कहुँ सीस चढ़ाए। एक एक के कोटिन्ह पाए।।
verses.transliteration: Re kubhāgya saṭha manda kubuddhe, taiṁ sura nara muni nāga biruddhe. Sādara siva kahum̐ sīsa caḍhāe, eka eka ke koṭinha pāe.
verses.translation: 'O wretched, foolish, dull-witted sinner! You have opposed gods, men, sages, and serpents. You devoutly offered heads to Shiva and received millions of boons for each one.'
verses.hindiTranslation: (विभीषण ने रावण से कहा) अरे कुभाग्य! मूर्ख! मन्द! कुबुद्धि! तूने देवता, मनुष्य, मुनि, नाग — सबसे विरोध किया। आदरपूर्वक शिव को सिर अर्पित किए।

verses.original:
तेहि कारन खल अब लगि बाँच्यो। अब तव कालु सीस पर नाच्यो।।
राम बिमुख सठ चहसि संपदा। अस कहि हनेसि माझ उर गदा।।
verses.transliteration: Tehi kārana khala aba lagi bām̐cyo, aba tava kālu sīsa para nācyo. Rāma bimukha saṭha cahasi sampadā, asa kahi hanesi mājha ura gadā.
verses.translation: 'That is why, O wretch, you have survived so long. But now Death dances on your head. You desire prosperity while turning away from Rama!' So saying, he struck him on the chest with the mace.
verses.hindiTranslation: (विभीषण ने कहा) उसी कारण दुष्ट अब तक बचा। अब तेरा काल सिर पर नाच रहा। श्रीराम-विमुख होकर मूर्ख सुख चाहता!

verses.original:
उर माझ गदा प्रहार घोर कठोर लागत महि पर् यो।
दस बदन सोनित स्त्रवत पुनि संभारि धायो रिस भर् यो।।
द्वौ भिरे अतिबल मल्लजुद्ध बिरुद्ध एकु एकहि हनै।
रघुबीर बल दर्पित बिभीषनु घालि नहिं ता कहुँ गनै।।
verses.transliteration: Ura mājha gadā prahāra ghora kaṭhora lāgata mahi paryo. Dasa badana sonita stravata puni sambhāri dhāyo risa bharyo. Dvau bhire atibala mallajuddha biruddha eku ekahi hanai. Raghubīra bala darpita bibhīṣanu ghāli nahiṁ tā kahum̐ ganai.
verses.translation: The mace struck a fierce, heavy blow upon his chest and he fell to the ground. Blood streamed from his ten mouths, but he gathered himself and charged back, filled with fury. The two fought a mighty wrestling match, striking each other blow for blow. Emboldened by Raghubir's strength, Vibhishana flung him down and thought nothing of it.
verses.hindiTranslation: वक्ष पर गदा का घोर-कठोर प्रहार लगा — पृथ्वी पर गिरा। दसों मुखों से रुधिर बहता — फिर सँभलकर उठा।

verses.original:
उमा बिभीषनु रावनहि सन्मुख चितव कि काउ।
सो अब भिरत काल ज्यों श्रीरघुबीर प्रभाउ।।94।।
verses.transliteration: Umā bibhīṣanu rāvanahi sanmukha citava ki kāu. So aba bhirata kāla jyoṁ śrīraghubīra prabhāu.
verses.translation: Uma, could Vibhishana ever have dared to face Ravana? Yet now he fights like Death itself — such is the glory of Sri Raghubir.
verses.hindiTranslation: उमा! विभीषण रावण के सामने देखता — कौन? (पहले कभी नहीं देख सकता था।) अब काल-सा भिड़ता — श्रीरघुवीर प्रभु के प्रताप से।

verses.original:
देखा श्रमित बिभीषनु भारी। धायउ हनूमान गिरि धारी।।
रथ तुरंग सारथी निपाता। हृदय माझ तेहि मारेसि लाता।।
verses.transliteration: Dekhā śramita bibhīṣanu bhārī, dhāyau hanūmāna giri dhārī. Ratha turaṅga sārathī nipātā, hṛdaya mājha tehi māresi lātā.
verses.translation: Seeing Vibhishana greatly exhausted, Hanuman rushed forth bearing a mountain. He destroyed Ravana's chariot, horses, and charioteer, then kicked him hard in the chest.
verses.hindiTranslation: विभीषण को अत्यन्त श्रमित देखकर हनुमान पर्वत लेकर दौड़ा। रथ, घोड़े, सारथी गिराए। हृदय पर (मुक्का मारा)।

verses.original:
ठाढ़ रहा अति कंपित गाता। गयउ बिभीषनु जहँ जनत्राता।।
पुनि रावन कपि हतेउ पचारी। चलेउ गगन कपि पूँछ पसारी।।
verses.transliteration: Ṭhāḍha rahā ati kampita gātā, gayau bibhīṣanu jaham̐ janatrātā. Puni rāvana kapi hateu pacārī, caleu gagana kapi pūm̐cha pasārī.
verses.translation: Ravana stood with his body trembling violently. Vibhishana withdrew to where the Lord was. Then Ravana challenged and struck the monkey; Hanuman spread his tail and flew into the sky.
verses.hindiTranslation: (रावण) खड़ा रहा — अत्यन्त काँपता शरीर। विभीषण जनत्राता (श्रीराम) के पास गया। फिर रावण ने वानर को मारा — ललकारकर।

verses.original:
गहिसि पूँछ कपि सहित उड़ाना। पुनि फिरि भिरेउ प्रबल हनुमाना।।
लरत अकास जुगल सम जोधा। एकहि एकु हनत करि क्रोधा।।
verses.transliteration: Gahisi pūm̐cha kapi sahita uḍānā, puni phiri bhireu prabala hanumānā. Larata akāsa jugala sama jodhā, ekahi eku hanata kari krodhā.
verses.translation: Ravana seized his tail, but the monkey flew with him. Then mighty Hanuman turned back to fight. The two equal warriors battled in the sky, each striking the other in rage.
verses.hindiTranslation: (रावण ने) पूँछ पकड़कर कपि सहित उड़ा। फिर प्रबल हनुमान लौटकर भिड़ा। आकाश में दोनों लड़ते — शूरवीर।

verses.original:
सोहहिं नभ छल बल बहु करहीं। कज्जल गिरि सुमेरु जनु लरहीं।।
बुधि बल निसिचर परइ न पार् यो। तब मारुत सुत प्रभु संभार् यो।।
verses.transliteration: Sohahi nabha chala bala bahu karahīṁ, kajjala giri sumeru janu larahīṁ. Budhi bala nisicara parai na pāryo, taba māruta suta prabhu sambhāryo.
verses.translation: They looked splendid in the sky, using many tricks and devices — as though Mount Kajjal and Mount Sumeru were fighting. Despite all his cunning and strength, the demon could not prevail. Then Hanuman remembered his Lord.
verses.hindiTranslation: आकाश में शोभित — बहुत छल-बल करते। मानो काजल-पर्वत और सुमेरु लड़ रहे। बुद्धि-बल से राक्षस वानर पर भारी पड़ने लगा।

verses.original:
संभारि श्रीरघुबीर धीर पचारि कपि रावनु हन्यो।
महि परत पुनि उठि लरत देवन्ह जुगल कहुँ जय जय भन्यो।।
हनुमंत संकट देखि मर्कट भालु क्रोधातुर चले।
रन मत्त रावन सकल सुभट प्रचंड भुज बल दलमले।।
verses.transliteration: Sambhāri śrīraghubīra dhīra pacāri kapi rāvanu hanyo. Mahi parata puni uṭhi larata devanha jugala kahum̐ jaya jaya bhanyo. Hanumanta saṅkaṭa dekhi markaṭa bhālu krodhātura cale. Rana matta rāvana sakala subhaṭa pracaṇḍa bhuja bala dalamale.
verses.translation: Remembering Sri Raghubir, the steadfast Hanuman challenged and struck Ravana. He fell to the earth but rose to fight again. The gods cried 'Victory!' to both. Seeing Hanuman in peril, monkeys and bears rushed forth in fury. But Ravana, intoxicated with battle, crushed all the warriors with the might of his arms.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीरघुवीर का स्मरण करके, धीरज धारकर, ललकारकर — कपि ने रावण को मारा। पृथ्वी पर गिरता — फिर उठकर लड़ता — देवताओं ने युद्ध देखा।

verses.original:
तब रघुबीर पचारे धाए कीस प्रचंड।
कपि बल प्रबल देखि तेहिं कीन्ह प्रगट पाषंड।।95।।
verses.transliteration: Taba raghubīra pacāre dhāe kīsa pracaṇḍa. Kapi bala prabala dekhi tehi kīnha pragaṭa pāṣaṇḍa.
verses.translation: Then Raghubir rallied his forces, and fierce monkeys charged forth. Seeing the monkeys' great strength, Ravana resorted to open sorcery.
verses.hindiTranslation: तब श्रीरघुवीर ने पुकारा — प्रचण्ड वानर दौड़े। कपि-बल प्रबल देखकर उसने (रावण ने) प्रकट किया पाखण्ड (माया)।

verses.original:
अंतरधान भयउ छन एका। पुनि प्रगटे खल रूप अनेका।।
रघुपति कटक भालु कपि जेते। जहँ तहँ प्रगट दसानन तेते।।
verses.transliteration: Antaradhāna bhayau chana ekā, puni pragaṭe khala rūpa anekā. Raghupati kaṭaka bhālu kapi jete, jaham̐ taham̐ pragaṭa dasānana tete.
verses.translation: In an instant he vanished, then reappeared in many forms. As many monkeys and bears were in Raghupati's army, that many Ravanas appeared everywhere.
verses.hindiTranslation: एक क्षण अन्तर्धान हुआ। फिर दुष्ट ने अनेक रूप प्रकट किए। श्रीरघुपति की सेना में जितने भालू-वानर — सबके जहाँ-तहाँ —

verses.original:
देखे कपिन्ह अमित दससीसा। जहँ तहँ भजे भालु अरु कीसा।।
भागे बानर धरहिं न धीरा। त्राहि त्राहि लछिमन रघुबीरा।।
verses.transliteration: Dekhe kapinha amita dasasīsā, jaham̐ taham̐ bhaje bhālu aru kīsā. Bhāge bānara dharahi na dhīrā, trāhi trāhi lachimana raghubīrā.
verses.translation: The monkeys saw countless ten-headed ones everywhere. Bears and monkeys fled in all directions, unable to maintain their courage, crying 'Save us! Save us, Lakshmana! Raghubir!'
verses.hindiTranslation: वानरों ने अमित (अनगिनत) दशमुख देखे — जहाँ-तहाँ भालू और वानर भागे। भागे — धीरज धारण नहीं करते।

verses.original:
दहँ दिसि धावहिं कोटिन्ह रावन। गर्जहिं घोर कठोर भयावन।।
डरे सकल सुर चले पराई। जय कै आस तजहु अब भाई।।
verses.transliteration: Daham̐ disi dhāvahi koṭinha rāvana, garjahi ghora kaṭhora bhayāvana. Ḍare sakala sura cale parāī, jaya kai āsa tajahu aba bhāī.
verses.translation: Millions of Ravanas ran in all ten directions, roaring horribly and terrifyingly. All the gods fled in fear: 'Abandon hope of victory now, brothers!'
verses.hindiTranslation: दसों दिशाओं में करोड़ों रावण दौड़ते — गरजते, घोर, कठोर, भयावन। सब देवता डरे — भागने लगे।

verses.original:
सब सुर जिते एक दसकंधर। अब बहु भए तकहु गिरि कंदर।।
रहे बिरंचि संभु मुनि ग्यानी। जिन्ह जिन्ह प्रभु महिमा कछु जानी।।
verses.transliteration: Saba sura jite eka dasakandhara, aba bahu bhae takahu giri kandara. Rahe birañci sambhu muni gyānī, jinha jinha prabhu mahimā kachu jānī.
verses.translation: 'One Ravana alone had conquered all the gods. Now that there are many, seek refuge in mountain caves!' But Brahma, Shiva, and the wise sages who knew something of the Lord's glory remained unafraid.
verses.hindiTranslation: (देवताओं ने कहा) एक दशकंधर ने सब देवताओं को जीता। अब बहुत हो गए — पर्वत-गुफाओं में छिपो। ब्रह्मा, शिव और मुनि ज्ञानी थे — (इसलिए) रुके।

verses.original:
जाना प्रताप ते रहे निर्भय कपिन्ह रिपु माने फुरे।
चले बिचलि मर्कट भालु सकल कृपाल पाहि भयातुरे।।
हनुमंत अंगद नील नल अतिबल लरत रन बाँकुरे।
मर्दहिं दसानन कोटि कोटिन्ह कपट भू भट अंकुरे।।
verses.transliteration: Jānā pratāpa te rahe nirbhaya kapinha ripu māne phure. Cale bicali markaṭa bhālu sakala kṛpāla pāhi bhayāture. Hanumanta aṅgada nīla nala atibala larata rana bām̐kure. Mardahi dasānana koṭi koṭinha kapaṭa bhū bhaṭa aṅkure.
verses.translation: Those who knew the Lord's glory remained fearless. The monkeys, however, took the illusory forms to be real. Bewildered, all the monkeys and bears fled, crying 'O merciful Lord, protect us!' But Hanuman, Angad, Nila, Nala, and other mighty warriors fought on bravely, crushing millions of illusory Ravanas sprouting from the earth.
verses.hindiTranslation: (ज्ञानियों ने) प्रताप से जाना — निर्भय रहे। वानरों ने शत्रु को सच्चा माना। सब वानर-भालू विचलित होकर चले।

verses.original:
सुर बानर देखे बिकल हँस्यो कोसलाधीस।
सजि सारंग एक सर हते सकल दससीस।।96।।
verses.transliteration: Sura bānara dekhe bikala ham̐syo kosalādhīsa. Saji sāraṅga eka sara hate sakala dasasīsa.
verses.translation: Seeing the gods and monkeys distressed, the Lord of Kosala smiled. Readying his Sharanga bow with a single arrow, he destroyed all the illusory ten-headed ones.
verses.hindiTranslation: देवता और वानर व्याकुल देखकर कोसलाधीश हँसे। सारंग (धनुष) सजाकर एक बाण से सब दशमुख मारे।

verses.original:
प्रभु छन महुँ माया सब काटी। जिमि रबि उएँ जाहिं तम फाटी।।
रावनु एकु देखि सुर हरषे। फिरे सुमन बहु प्रभु पर बरषे।।
verses.transliteration: Prabhu chana mahum̐ māyā saba kāṭī, jimi rabi uem̐ jāhi tama phāṭī. Rāvanu eku dekhi sura haraṣe, phire sumana bahu prabhu para baraṣe.
verses.translation: In an instant the Lord dispelled all the illusion, just as darkness vanishes at sunrise. The gods rejoiced to see only one Ravana and showered many flowers upon the Lord.
verses.hindiTranslation: प्रभु ने क्षण-भर में सारी माया काट दी — जैसे सूर्य उदय होने पर अन्धकार भागता है। एक रावण देखकर देवता —

verses.original:
भुज उठाइ रघुपति कपि फेरे। फिरे एक एकन्ह तब टेरे।।
प्रभु बलु पाइ भालु कपि धाए। तरल तमकि संजुग महि आए।।
verses.transliteration: Bhuja uṭhāi raghupati kapi phere, phire eka ekanha taba ṭere. Prabhu balu pāi bhālu kapi dhāe, tarala tamaki sañjuga mahi āe.
verses.translation: Raising his arm, Raghupati rallied the monkeys. They turned back, calling to one another. Receiving the Lord's strength, the bears and monkeys charged forth, trembling with eagerness, and entered the battlefield.

verses.original:
अस्तुति करत देवतन्हि देखें। भयउँ एक मैं इन्ह के लेखें।।
सठहु सदा तुम्ह मोर मरायल। अस कहि कोपि गगन पर धायल।।
verses.transliteration: Astuti karata devatanhi dekhēṁ, bhayaum̐ eka maiṁ inha ke lekhēṁ. Saṭhahu sadā tumha mora marāyala, asa kahi kopi gagana para dhāyala.
verses.translation: Seeing the gods praising him, Ravana thought, 'In their estimation I have become just one!' 'Fools! You have always been my prey!' So saying, he charged up into the sky in rage.

verses.original:
हाहाकार करत सुर भागे। खलहु जाहु कहँ मोरें आगे।।
देखि बिकल सुर अंगद धायो। कूदि चरन गहि भूमि गिरायो।।
verses.transliteration: Hāhākāra karata sura bhāge, khalahu jāhu kaham̐ morēṁ āge. Dekhi bikala sura aṅgada dhāyo, kūdi carana gahi bhūmi girāyo.
verses.translation: The gods fled with cries of despair. 'Where will you wretches go from me?' Seeing the gods in distress, Angad leaped up, seized Ravana by the feet, and flung him to the ground.

verses.original:
गहि भूमि पार् यो लात मार् यो बालिसुत प्रभु पहिं गयो।
संभारि उठि दसकंठ घोर कठोर रव गर्जत भयो।।
करि दाप चाप चढ़ाइ दस संधानि सर बहु बरषई।
किए सकल भट घायल भयाकुल देखि निज बल हरषई।।
verses.transliteration: Gahi bhūmi pāryo lāta māryo bālisuta prabhu pahi gayo. Sambhāri uṭhi dasakaṇṭha ghora kaṭhora rava garjata bhayo. Kari dāpa cāpa caḍhāi dasa sandhāni sara bahu baraṣaī. Kie sakala bhaṭa ghāyala bhayākula dekhi nija bala haraṣaī.
verses.translation: He seized him and dashed him to the ground, kicked him, and then Vali's son returned to the Lord. Gathering himself, the ten-headed one roared in a deep, terrible voice. Boasting, he strung his ten bows, fitted arrows, and rained them forth. He wounded all the warriors and, seeing his own strength, rejoiced.

verses.original:
तब रघुपति रावन के सीस भुजा सर चाप।
काटे बहुत बढ़े पुनि जिमि तीरथ कर पाप। 97।।
verses.transliteration: Taba raghupati rāvana ke sīsa bhujā sara cāpa. Kāṭe bahuta baḍhe puni jimi tīratha kara pāpa.
verses.translation: Then Raghupati cut Ravana's heads, arms, arrows, and bows — but they grew back in profusion, just as sin committed at a holy place multiplies.

verses.original:
सिर भुज बाढ़ि देखि रिपु केरी। भालु कपिन्ह रिस भई घनेरी।।
मरत न मूढ़ कटेउ भुज सीसा। धाए कोपि भालु भट कीसा।।
verses.transliteration: Sira bhuja bāḍhi dekhi ripu kerī, bhālu kapinha risa bhaī ghanerī. Marata na mūḍha kaṭeu bhuja sīsā, dhāe kopi bhālu bhaṭa kīsā.
verses.translation: Seeing the enemy's heads and arms multiply, the bears and monkeys grew deeply angry. 'The fool does not die even when heads and arms are cut!' The bears and monkey warriors charged in fury.

verses.original:
बालितनय मारुति नल नीला। बानरराज दुबिद बलसीला।।
बिटप महीधर करहिं प्रहारा। सोइ गिरि तरु गहि कपिन्ह सो मारा।।
verses.transliteration: Bālitanaya māruti nala nīlā, bānarārāja dubida balasīlā. Biṭapa mahīdhara karahi prahārā, soi giri taru gahi kapinha so mārā.
verses.translation: Vali's son, Hanuman, Nala, Nila, the monkey king, and Dvivida — all mighty warriors — struck with trees and mountains. But Ravana seized those same trees and mountains and hurled them back at the monkeys.

verses.original:
एक नखन्हि रिपु बपुष बिदारी। भअगि चलहिं एक लातन्ह मारी।।
तब नल नील सिरन्हि चढ़ि गयऊ। नखन्हि लिलार बिदारत भयऊ।।
verses.transliteration: Eka nakhanhi ripu bapuṣa bidārī, bhagi calahi eka lātanha mārī. Taba nala nīla siranha caḍhi gayaū, nakhanhi lilāra bidārata bhayaū.
verses.translation: Some tore his body with their claws and others fled after kicking him. Then Nala and Nila climbed upon his heads and began clawing his foreheads.

verses.original:
रुधिर देखि बिषाद उर भारी। तिन्हहि धरन कहुँ भुजा पसारी।।
गहे न जाहिं करन्हि पर फिरहीं। जनु जुग मधुप कमल बन चरहीं।।
verses.transliteration: Rudhira dekhi biṣāda ura bhārī, tinahi dharana kahum̐ bhujā pasārī. Gahe na jāhi karanhi para phirahīṁ, janu juga madhupa kamala bana carahīṁ.
verses.translation: Seeing the blood, he was filled with great distress. He spread his arms to seize them, but they could not be caught — they kept leaping over his hands, like two bees flitting through a lotus garden.

verses.original:
कोपि कूदि द्वौ धरेसि बहोरी। महि पटकत भजे भुजा मरोरी।।
पुनि सकोप दस धनु कर लीन्हे। सरन्हि मारि घायल कपि कीन्हे।।
verses.transliteration: Kopi kūdi dvau dharesi bahorī, mahi paṭakata bhaje bhujā marorī. Puni sakopa dasa dhanu kara līnhe, saranha māri ghāyala kapi kīnhe.
verses.translation: In rage he finally caught them both and dashed them to the ground, wringing their arms. Then angrily he took up ten bows and wounded the monkeys with showers of arrows.

verses.original:
हनुमदादि मुरुछित करि बंदर। पाइ प्रदोष हरष दसकंधर।।
मुरुछित देखि सकल कपि बीरा। जामवंत धायउ रनधीरा।।
verses.transliteration: Hanumadādi muruchita kari bandara, pāi pradoṣa haraṣa dasakandhara. Muruchita dekhi sakala kapi bīrā, jāmavanta dhāyau ranadhīrā.
verses.translation: He rendered Hanuman and the other monkeys unconscious. As twilight fell, the ten-headed one rejoiced. Seeing all the monkey heroes unconscious, Jambavan, steady in battle, charged forth.

verses.original:
संग भालु भूधर तरु धारी। मारन लगे पचारि पचारी।।
भयउ क्रुद्ध रावन बलवाना। गहि पद महि पटकइ भट नाना।।
verses.transliteration: Saṅga bhālu bhūdhara taru dhārī, mārana lage pacāri pacārī. Bhayau kruddha rāvana balavānā, gahi pada mahi paṭakai bhaṭa nānā.
verses.translation: With bears carrying mountains and trees, they began striking and challenging him repeatedly. Ravana grew furious and mighty; he seized warriors by their feet and dashed them on the ground.

verses.original: देखि भालुपति निज दल घाता। कोपि माझ उर मारेसि लाता।।
verses.transliteration: Dekhi bhālupati nija dala ghātā, kopi mājha ura māresi lātā.
verses.translation: Seeing his own army being slaughtered, the bear-chief angrily kicked Ravana in the chest.

verses.original:
उर लात घात प्रचंड लागत बिकल रथ ते महि परा।
गहि भालु बीसहुँ कर मनहुँ कमलन्हि बसे निसि मधुकरा।।
मुरुछित बिलोकि बहोरि पद हति भालुपति प्रभु पहिं गयौ।
निसि जानि स्यंदन घालि तेहि तब सूत जतनु करत भयो।।
verses.transliteration: Ura lāta ghāta pracaṇḍa lāgata bikala ratha te mahi parā. Gahi bhālu bīsahum̐ kara manahum̐ kamalanha base nisi madhukara. Muruchita biloki bahori pada hati bhālupati prabhu pahi gayau. Nisi jāni syandana ghāli tehi taba sūta jatanu karata bhayo.
verses.translation: The fierce kick struck his chest, and he fell stunned from his chariot to the ground. The bear clung to all twenty of his hands like bees settled on lotuses at night. Seeing him unconscious, the bear-chief kicked him again and returned to the Lord. Then his charioteer, knowing it was night, placed him on the chariot and began tending to him.

verses.original:
मुरुछा बिगत भालु कपि सब आए प्रभु पास।
निसिचर सकल रावनहि घेरि रहे अति त्रास।।98।।
verses.transliteration: Muruchā bigata bhālu kapi saba āe prabhu pāsa. Nisicara sakala rāvanahi gheri rahe ati trāsa.
verses.translation: When the fainting passed, all the bears and monkeys came to the Lord. All the demons surrounded Ravana, trembling with great fear.

verses.original:
तेही निसि सीता पहिं जाई। त्रिजटा कहि सब कथा सुनाई।।
सिर भुज बाढ़ि सुनत रिपु केरी। सीता उर भइ त्रास घनेरी।।
verses.transliteration: Tehī nisi sītā pahi jāī, trijaṭā kahi saba kathā sunāī. Sira bhuja bāḍhi sunata ripu kerī, sītā ura bhai trāsa ghanerī.
verses.translation: That night, Trijata went to Sita and told her the whole story. Hearing that the enemy's heads and arms kept regrowing, Sita was filled with great terror.

verses.original:
मुख मलीन उपजी मन चिंता। त्रिजटा सन बोली तब सीता।।
होइहि कहा कहसि किन माता। केहि बिधि मरिहि बिस्व दुखदाता।।
verses.transliteration: Mukha malīna upajī mana cintā, trijaṭā sana bolī taba sītā. Hoihi kahā kahasi kina mātā, kehi bidhi marihi bisva dukhādātā.
verses.translation: Her face grew pale and worry arose in her mind. Then Sita spoke to Trijata: 'What will happen? Why don't you tell me, mother? By what means will this tormentor of the world die?'

verses.original:
रघुपति सर सिर कटेहुँ न मरई। बिधि बिपरीत चरित सब करई।।
मोर अभाग्य जिआवत ओही। जेहिं हौ हरि पद कमल बिछोही।।
verses.transliteration: Raghupati sara sira kaṭehum̐ na maraī, bidhi biparīta carita saba karaī. Mora abhāgya jiāvata ohī, jehi hau hari pada kamala bichohī.
verses.translation: 'Raghupati's arrows cut his heads but he does not die! Fate does everything in reverse. My misfortune keeps him alive — he who has separated me from Lord Hari's lotus feet.'

verses.original:
जेहिं कृत कपट कनक मृग झूठा। अजहुँ सो दैव मोहि पर रूठा।।
जेहिं बिधि मोहि दुख दुसह सहाए। लछिमन कहुँ कटु बचन कहाए।।
verses.transliteration: Jehi kṛta kapaṭa kanaka mṛga jhūṭhā, ajahum̐ so daiva mohi para rūṭhā. Jehi bidhi mohi dukha dusaha sahāe, lachimana kahum̐ kaṭu bacana kahāe.
verses.translation: 'The same fate that created the false golden deer — that destiny is still angry with me. The same providence that made me suffer unbearable woes and forced me to speak harsh words to Lakshmana.'

verses.original:
रघुपति बिरह सबिष सर भारी। तकि तकि मार बार बहु मारी।।
ऐसेहुँ दुख जो राख मम प्राना। सोइ बिधि ताहि जिआव न आना।।
verses.transliteration: Raghupati biraha sabiṣa sara bhārī, taki taki māra bāra bahu mārī. Aisehum̐ dukha jo rākha mama prānā, soi bidhi tāhi jiāva na ānā.
verses.translation: 'Separation from Raghupati has struck me again and again like poisoned arrows. The same fate that preserved my life through such suffering — that same fate now keeps him alive.'

verses.original:
बहु बिधि कर बिलाप जानकी। करि करि सुरति कृपानिधान की।।
कह त्रिजटा सुनु राजकुमारी। उर सर लागत मरइ सुरारी।।
verses.transliteration: Bahu bidhi kara bilāpa jānakī, kari kari surati kṛpānidhāna kī. Kaha trijaṭā sunu rājakumārī, ura sara lāgata marai surārī.
verses.translation: Janaki lamented in many ways, remembering the ocean of mercy. Trijata said, 'Listen, O princess: the enemy will die when an arrow strikes his chest.'

verses.original: प्रभु ताते उर हतइ न तेही। एहि के हृदयँ बसति बैदेही।।
verses.transliteration: Prabhu tāte ura hataī na tehī, ehi ke hṛdayam̐ basati baidehī.
verses.translation: 'The Lord does not strike his heart because Vaidehi (you) dwells within it.'

verses.original:
एहि के हृदयँ बस जानकी जानकी उर मम बास है।
मम उदर भुअन अनेक लागत बान सब कर नास है।।
सुनि बचन हरष बिषाद मन अति देखि पुनि त्रिजटाँ कहा।
अब मरिहि रिपु एहि बिधि सुनहि सुंदरि तजहि संसय महा।।
verses.transliteration: Ehi ke hṛdayam̐ basa jānakī jānakī ura mama bāsa hai. Mama udara bhuana aneka lāgata bāna saba kara nāsa hai. Suni bacana haraṣa biṣāda mana ati dekhi puni trijaṭām̐ kahā. Aba marihi ripu ehi bidhi sunahi sundarī tajahi sansaya mahā.
verses.translation: 'In his heart dwells Janaki, and in Janaki's heart is my abode. Within me are many universes — if the arrows strike, all would be destroyed.' Hearing these words, Sita felt both joy and sorrow. Then Trijata spoke again: 'Now the enemy shall die in this manner — listen, O beautiful one, and abandon your great doubt.'

verses.original:
काटत सिर होइहि बिकल छुटि जाइहि तव ध्यान।
तब रावनहि हृदय महुँ मरिहहिं रामु सुजान।।99।।
verses.transliteration: Kāṭata sira hoihi bikala chuṭi jāihi tava dhyāna. Taba rāvanahi hṛdaya mahum̐ marihahi rāmu sujāna.
verses.translation: 'When his heads are cut, he will become distracted and your image will leave his mind. Then wise Rama will strike his heart and slay him.'

verses.original:
अस कहि बहुत भाँति समुझाई। पुनि त्रिजटा निज भवन सिधाई।।
राम सुभाउ सुमिरि बैदेही। उपजी बिरह बिथा अति तेही।।
verses.transliteration: Asa kahi bahuta bhām̐ti samujhāī, puni trijaṭā nija bhavana sidhāī. Rāma subhāu sumiri baidehī, upajī biraha bithā ati tehī.
verses.translation: So saying, Trijata consoled Sita in many ways and then returned to her own home. Remembering Rama's gentle nature, Vaidehi was overcome with the pain of separation.

verses.original:
निसिहि ससिहि निंदति बहु भाँती। जुग सम भई सिराति न राती।।
करति बिलाप मनहिं मन भारी। राम बिरहँ जानकी दुखारी।।
verses.transliteration: Nisihi sasihi nindati bahu bhām̐tī, juga sama bhaī sirāti na rātī. Karati bilāpa manahi mana bhārī, rāma biraham̐ jānakī dukhārī.
verses.translation: She cursed the night and the moon in many ways. The night felt as long as an age and would not end. Janaki lamented silently in her mind, suffering the pain of Rama's separation.

verses.original:
जब अति भयउ बिरह उर दाहू। फरकेउ बाम नयन अरु बाहू।।
सगुन बिचारि धरी मन धीरा। अब मिलिहहिं कृपाल रघुबीरा।।
verses.transliteration: Jaba ati bhayau biraha ura dāhū, pharakeu bāma nayana aru bāhū. Saguna bicāri dharī mana dhīrā, aba milihahi kṛpāla raghubīrā.
verses.translation: When the fire of separation blazed fiercely in her heart, her left eye and arm throbbed. Reflecting on this auspicious omen, she took courage: 'Now the merciful Raghubir will meet me.'

verses.original:
इहाँ अर्धनिसि रावनु जागा। निज सारथि सन खीझन लागा।।
सठ रनभूमि छड़ाइसि मोही। धिग धिग अधम मंदमति तोही।।
verses.transliteration: Ihām̐ ardhanisi rāvanu jāgā, nija sārathi sana khījhana lāgā. Saṭha ranabhūmi chaḍāisi mohī, dhig dhig adhama mandamati tohī.
verses.translation: Here, Ravana woke at midnight and began berating his charioteer: 'Fool, you carried me away from the battlefield! Shame, shame, you base, dull-witted wretch!'

verses.original:
तेहिं पद गहि बहु बिधि समुझावा। भौरु भएँ रथ चढ़ि पुनि धावा।।
सुनि आगवनु दसानन केरा। कपि दल खरभर भयउ घनेरा।।
verses.transliteration: Tehi pada gahi bahu bidhi samujhāvā, bhauru bhaem̐ ratha caḍhi puni dhāvā. Suni āgavanu dasānana kerā, kapi dala kharabhara bhayau ghanerā.
verses.translation: The charioteer clasped his feet and consoled him in many ways. At dawn, Ravana mounted his chariot and charged forth again. Hearing of Ravana's approach, there was great commotion in the monkey army.

verses.original: जहँ तहँ भूधर बिटप उपारी। धाए कटकटाइ भट भारी।।
verses.transliteration: Jaham̐ taham̐ bhūdhara biṭapa upārī, dhāe kaṭakaṭāi bhaṭa bhārī.
verses.translation: Here and there, the mighty warriors uprooted boulders and trees and rushed forth, grinding their teeth.

verses.original:
धाए जो मर्कट बिकट भालु कराल कर भूधर धरा।
अति कोप करहिं प्रहार मारत भजि चले रजनीचरा।।
बिचलाइ दल बलवंत कीसन्ह घेरि पुनि रावनु लियो।
चहुँ दिसि चपेटन्हि मारि नखन्हि बिदारि तनु ब्याकुल कियो।।
verses.transliteration: Dhāe jo markaṭa bikaṭa bhālu karāla kara bhūdhara dharā. Ati kopa karahi prahāra mārata bhaji cale rajanīcarā. Bicalāi dala balavanta kīsanha gheri puni rāvanu liyo. Cahum̐ disi capeṭanhi māri nakhanhi bidāri tanu byākula kiyo.
verses.translation: The fierce monkeys and terrible bears charged with mountains in hand. In great fury they struck, and the demons fled. Routing the army, the mighty monkeys then surrounded Ravana. From all four sides they struck him with slaps and clawed his body, leaving him utterly distraught.

verses.original:
देखि महा मर्कट प्रबल रावन कीन्ह बिचार।
अंतरहित होइ निमिष महुँ कृत माया बिस्तार।।100।।
verses.transliteration: Dekhi mahā markaṭa prabala rāvana kīnha bicāra. Antarahita hoi nimiṣa mahum̐ kṛta māyā bistāra.
verses.translation: Seeing the monkeys' great prowess, Ravana deliberated. In an instant he vanished and spread his vast illusion.

verses.original:
जब कीन्ह तेहिं पाषंड। भए प्रगट जंतु प्रचंड।।
बेताल भूत पिसाच। कर धरें धनु नाराच।।1।।
जोगिनि गहें करबाल। एक हाथ मनुज कपाल।।
करि सद्य सोनित पान। नाचहिं करहिं बहु गान।।2।।
धरु मारु बोलहिं घोर। रहि पूरि धुनि चहुँ ओर।।
मुख बाइ धावहिं खान। तब लगे कीस परान।।3।।
जहँ जाहिं मर्कट भागि। तहँ बरत देखहिं आगि।।
भए बिकल बानर भालु। पुनि लाग बरषै बालु।।4।।
जहँ तहँ थकित करि कीस। गर्जेउ बहुरि दससीस।।
लछिमन कपीस समेत। भए सकल बीर अचेत।।5।।
हा राम हा रघुनाथ। कहि सुभट मीजहिं हाथ।।
एहि बिधि सकल बल तोरि। तेहिं कीन्ह कपट बहोरि।।6।।
प्रगटेसि बिपुल हनुमान। धाए गहे पाषान।।
तिन्ह रामु घेरे जाइ। चहुँ दिसि बरूथ बनाइ।।7।।
मारहु धरहु जनि जाइ। कटकटहिं पूँछ उठाइ।।
दहँ दिसि लँगूर बिराज। तेहिं मध्य कोसलराज।।8।।
तेहिं मध्य कोसलराज सुंदर स्याम तन सोभा लही।
जनु इंद्रधनुष अनेक की बर बारि तुंग तमालही।।
प्रभु देखि हरष बिषाद उर सुर बदत जय जय जय करी।
रघुबीर एकहि तीर कोपि निमेष महुँ माया हरी।।1।।
माया बिगत कपि भालु हरषे बिटप गिरि गहि सब फिरे।
सर निकर छाड़े राम रावन बाहु सिर पुनि महि गिरे।।
श्रीराम रावन समर चरित अनेक कल्प जो गावहीं।
सत सेष सारद निगम कबि तेउ तदपि पार न पावहीं।।2।।
verses.transliteration: Jaba kīnha tehi pāṣaṇḍa, bhae pragaṭa jantu pracaṇḍa... (extensive chhand)
verses.translation: When he wrought that sorcery, fierce creatures appeared — vampires, ghosts, goblins carrying bows and arrows. Yoginis wielded swords, holding human skulls in one hand. Drinking fresh blood, they danced and sang. 'Seize! Kill!' — terrible cries filled every direction. They ran with gaping mouths to devour; then the monkeys began to lose their lives. Wherever monkeys fled, they saw blazing fire. Monkeys and bears were distressed; then sand began to rain. Having exhausted the monkeys everywhere, the ten-headed one roared again. Lakshmana, the monkey king, and all the warriors lost consciousness. 'O Rama! O Raghunath!' the warriors cried, wringing their hands. Having thus broken all their strength, he created another illusion. He manifested many Hanumans who rushed forth with boulders. They surrounded Rama on all sides, forming a cordon. 'Strike! Seize! Don't let him escape!' they shriek, raising their tails. On all sides langurs shine, and in their midst stands the King of Kosala. In their midst, the King of Kosala, dark-bodied, shines with beauty — like a tall tamala tree amid many a splendid rainbow. Seeing the Lord, the gods felt both joy and sorrow, crying 'Victory! Victory! Victory!' Raghubir, in rage, in the blink of an eye, destroyed the illusion with a single arrow. With the illusion destroyed, the monkeys and bears rejoiced, and seizing trees and mountains, they all rallied. Rama released a volley of arrows, and Ravana's arms and heads fell to the ground again. The battle exploits of Sri Rama and Ravana — even if told across infinite ages — a hundred Sheshas, Sharadas, Vedas, and poets could not reach their end.

verses.original:
ताके गुन गन कछु कहे जड़मति तुलसीदास।
जिमि निज बल अनुरूप ते माछी उड़इ अकास।।101(क)।।
काटे सिर भुज बार बहु मरत न भट लंकेस।
प्रभु क्रीड़त सुर सिद्ध मुनि ब्याकुल देखि कलेस।।101(ख)।।
verses.transliteration: Tāke guna gana kachu kahe jaḍamati tulasīdāsa. Jimi nija bala anurūpa te māchī uḍai akāsa. Kāṭe sira bhuja bāra bahu marata na bhaṭa laṅkesa. Prabhu krīḍata sura siddha muni byākula dekhi kalesa.
verses.translation: Some of these glories were told by the dim-witted Tulsidas, as best as he could — like a fly attempting to fly through the sky. Rama cut heads and arms many times, yet the warrior king of Lanka did not die. The Lord played on while gods, siddhas, and sages watched in distress.

verses.original:
काटत बढ़हिं सीस समुदाई। जिमि प्रति लाभ लोभ अधिकाई।।
मरइ न रिपु श्रम भयउ बिसेषा। राम बिभीषन तन तब देखा।।
verses.transliteration: Kāṭata baḍhahi sīsa samudāī, jimi prati lābha lobha adhikāī. Marai na ripu śrama bhayau biseṣā, rāma bibhīṣana tana taba dekhā.
verses.translation: When cut, the heads multiply, just as greed increases with every gain. The enemy does not die; the Lord felt great exertion. Then Rama looked at Vibhishana.

verses.original:
उमा काल मर जाकीं ईछा। सो प्रभु जन कर प्रीति परीछा।।
सुनु सरबग्य चराचर नायक। प्रनतपाल सुर मुनि सुखदायक।।
verses.transliteration: Umā kāla mara jākīṁ īchā, so prabhu jana kara prīti parīchā. Sunu sarbagya carācara nāyaka, pranatapāla sura muni sukhadāyaka.
verses.translation: Uma, Death dies at whose mere wish — that Lord tests the devotion of his servant. 'Listen, O all-knowing Lord of the animate and inanimate, protector of the suppliant, giver of joy to gods and sages.'

verses.original:
नाभिकुंड पियूष बस याकें। नाथ जिअत रावनु बल ताकें।।
सुनत बिभीषन बचन कृपाला। हरषि गहे कर बान कराला।।
verses.transliteration: Nābhikuṇḍa piyūṣa basa yākēṁ, nātha jiata rāvanu bala tākēṁ. Sunata bibhīṣana bacana kṛpālā, haraṣi gahe kara bāna karālā.
verses.translation: 'In his navel resides a pool of nectar. By its power, Lord, Ravana remains alive.' Hearing Vibhishana's words, the merciful Lord joyfully took up his terrible arrows.

verses.original:
असुभ होन लागे तब नाना। रोवहिं खर सृकाल बहु स्वाना।।
बोलहि खग जग आरति हेतू। प्रगट भए नभ जहँ तहँ केतू।।
verses.transliteration: Asubha hona lāge taba nānā, rovahi khara sṛkāla bahu svānā. Bolahi khaga jaga ārati hetū, pragaṭa bhae nabha jaham̐ taham̐ ketū.
verses.translation: Then many inauspicious omens began to appear. Donkeys, jackals, and dogs howled. Birds cried out, portending the world's grief. Comets appeared in the sky here and there.

verses.original:
दस दिसि दाह होन अति लागा। भयउ परब बिनु रबि उपरागा।।
मंदोदरि उर कंपति भारी। प्रतिमा स्त्रवहिं नयन मग बारी।।
verses.transliteration: Dasa disi dāha hona ati lāgā, bhayau paraba binu rabi uparāgā. Mandodarī ura kampati bhārī, pratimā stravahi nayana maga bārī.
verses.translation: Fire blazed in all ten directions. An eclipse occurred without it being the appointed time. Mandodari's heart trembled violently. Temple images wept, shedding tears from their eyes.

verses.original:
प्रतिमा रुदहिं पबिपात नभ अति बात बह डोलति मही।
बरषहिं बलाहक रुधिर कच रज असुभ अति सक को कही।।
उतपात अमित बिलोकि नभ सुर बिकल बोलहि जय जए।
सुर सभय जानि कृपाल रघुपति चाप सर जोरत भए।।
verses.transliteration: Pratimā rudahi pabipāta nabha ati bāta baha ḍolati mahī. Baraṣahi balāhaka rudhira kaca raja asubha ati saka ko kahī. Utapāta amita biloki nabha sura bikala bolahi jaya jae. Sura sabhaya jāni kṛpāla raghupati cāpa sara jorata bhae.
verses.translation: Idols wept, thunderbolts fell, terrible winds blew, the earth shook. Clouds rained blood, hair, and dust — who can describe such inauspicious omens? Seeing countless portents in the sky, the anxious gods cried 'Victory! Victory!' Knowing the gods' fear, the compassionate Lord of the Raghu line began fitting arrows to his bow.

verses.original:
खैचि सरासन श्रवन लगि छाड़े सर एकतीस।
रघुनायक सायक चले मानहुँ काल फनीस।।102।।
verses.transliteration: Khaiṁci sarāsana śravana lagi chāḍe sara ekatīsa. Raghunāyaka sāyaka cale mānahum̐ kāla phanīsa.
verses.translation: Drawing the bow to his ear, he released thirty-one arrows. The arrows of Raghunayak flew forth like the serpents of Death.

verses.original:
सायक एक नाभि सर सोषा। अपर लगे भुज सिर करि रोषा।।
लै सिर बाहु चले नाराचा। सिर भुज हीन रुंड महि नाचा।।
verses.transliteration: Sāyaka eka nābhi sara soṣā, apara lage bhuja sira kari roṣā. Lai sira bāhu cale nārācā, sira bhuja hīna ruṇḍa mahi nācā.
verses.translation: One arrow dried up the pool of nectar in his navel. The others struck his arms and heads in anger. The arrows carried away his heads and arms; the headless, armless trunk danced on the ground.

verses.original:
धरनि धसइ धर धाव प्रचंडा। तब सर हति प्रभु कृत दुइ खंडा।।
गर्जेउ मरत घोर रव भारी। कहाँ रामु रन हतौं पचारी।।
verses.transliteration: Dharani dhasai dhara dhāva pracaṇḍā, taba sara hati prabhu kṛta dui khaṇḍā. Garjeu marata ghora rava bhārī, kahām̐ rāmu rana hataum̐ pacārī.
verses.translation: The trunk sank into the earth, then charged about fiercely. The Lord then cut it in two with another arrow. Dying, Ravana roared with a terrible cry: 'Where is Rama? I challenge him in battle!'

verses.original:
डोली भूमि गिरत दसकंधर। छुभित सिंधु सरि दिग्गज भूधर।।
धरनि परेउ द्वौ खंड बढ़ाई। चापि भालु मर्कट समुदाई।।
verses.transliteration: Ḍolī bhūmi girata dasakandhara, chubhita sindhu sari diggaja bhūdhara. Dharani pareu dvau khaṇḍa baḍhāī, cāpi bhālu markaṭa samudāī.
verses.translation: As the ten-headed one fell, the earth shook; oceans, rivers, elephants of the quarters, and mountains trembled. The earth expanded as the two halves of his body fell, crushing multitudes of bears and monkeys.

verses.original:
मंदोदरि आगें भुज सीसा। धरि सर चले जहाँ जगदीसा।।
प्रबिसे सब निषंग महु जाई। देखि सुरन्ह दुंदुभीं बजाई।।
verses.transliteration: Mandodarī āgēṁ bhuja sīsā, dhari sara cale jahām̐ jagadīsā. Prabise saba niṣaṅga mahu jāī, dekhi suranha dundubhīṁ bajāī.
verses.translation: His heads and arms were carried by the arrows to where Mandodari was, and then flew to where the Lord of the universe stood. All entered back into his quiver. Seeing this, the gods beat their drums.

verses.original:
तासु तेज समान प्रभु आनन। हरषे देखि संभु चतुरानन।।
जय जय धुनि पूरी ब्रह्मंडा। जय रघुबीर प्रबल भुजदंडा।।
verses.transliteration: Tāsu teja samāna prabhu ānana, haraṣe dekhi sambhu caturānana. Jaya jaya dhuni pūrī brahmaṇḍā, jaya raghubīra prabala bhujadaṇḍā.
verses.translation: Ravana's radiance merged into the Lord's face. Shiva and Brahma rejoiced to see it. Cries of 'Victory! Victory!' filled the universe: 'Victory to Raghubir of the mighty arms!'

verses.original: बरषहि सुमन देव मुनि बृंदा। जय कृपाल जय जयति मुकुंदा।।
verses.transliteration: Baraṣahi sumana deva muni bṛndā, jaya kṛpāla jaya jayati mukundā.
verses.translation: Hosts of gods and sages showered flowers: 'Glory to the merciful Lord! Glory, glory to Mukunda!'

verses.original:
जय कृपा कंद मुकंद द्वंद हरन सरन सुखप्रद प्रभो।
खल दल बिदारन परम कारन कारुनीक सदा बिभो।।
सुर सुमन बरषहिं हरष संकुल बाज दुंदुभि गहगही।
संग्राम अंगन राम अंग अनंग बहु सोभा लही।।
सिर जटा मुकुट प्रसून बिच बिच अति मनोहर राजहीं।
जनु नीलगिरि पर तड़ित पटल समेत उड़ुगन भ्राजहीं।।
भुजदंड सर कोदंड फेरत रुधिर कन तन अति बने।
जनु रायमुनीं तमाल पर बैठीं बिपुल सुख आपने।।
verses.transliteration: Jaya kṛpā kanda mukunda dvanda harana sarana sukhaprada prabho. Khala dala bidārana parama kārana kārunīka sadā bibho. Sura sumana baraṣahi haraṣa saṅkula bāja dundubhi gahagahī. Saṅgrāma aṅgana rāma aṅga anaṅga bahu sobhā lahī. Sira jaṭā mukuṭa prasūna bica bica ati manohara rājahīṁ. Janu nīlagiri para taḍita paṭala sameta uḍugana bhrājahīṁ. Bhujadaṇḍa sara kodaṇḍa pherata rudhira kana tana ati bane. Janu rāyamunīṁ tamāla para baiṭhīṁ bipula sukha āpane.
verses.translation: Victory to the root of mercy, Mukunda, destroyer of duality, giver of joy and refuge, O Lord! Slayer of the wicked host, the supreme cause, ever-compassionate, all-pervading Lord! The gods shower flowers in abundant joy; drums resound in great celebration. On the battlefield, Rama's body shines with the beauty of countless Cupids. The crown of matted locks with flowers strewn here and there looks most enchanting — like lightning and clusters of stars shining upon a dark mountain. His mighty arms twirl arrows and bow; drops of blood adorn his body beautifully — like many raayamuni birds perched in great delight upon a tamala tree.

verses.original:
कृपादृष्टि करि प्रभु अभय किए सुर बृंद।
भालु कीस सब हरषे जय सुख धाम मुकंद।।103।।
verses.transliteration: Kṛpādṛṣṭi kari prabhu abhaya kie sura bṛnda. Bhālu kīsa saba haraṣe jaya sukha dhāma mukunda.
verses.translation: With a compassionate glance, the Lord made all the gods fearless. All the bears and monkeys rejoiced: 'Glory to Mukunda, the abode of bliss!'

verses.original:
पति सिर देखत मंदोदरी। मुरुछित बिकल धरनि खसि परी।।
जुबति बृंद रोवत उठि धाईं। तेहि उठाइ रावन पहिं आई।।
verses.transliteration: Pati sira dekhata mandodarī, muruchita bikala dharani khasi parī. Jubati bṛnda rovata uṭhi dhāīṁ, tehi uṭhāi rāvana pahi āī.
verses.translation: Seeing her husband's severed heads, Mandodari fell to the ground in a faint, overcome with grief. Her women rose weeping and rushed forth; they lifted her and brought her to Ravana's side.

verses.original:
पति गति देखि ते करहिं पुकारा। छूटे कच नहिं बपुष सँभारा।।
उर ताड़ना करहिं बिधि नाना। रोवत करहिं प्रताप बखाना।।
verses.transliteration: Pati gati dekhi te karahi pukārā, chūṭe kaca nahi bapuṣa sam̐bhārā. Ura tāḍanā karahi bidhi nānā, rovata karahi pratāpa bakhānā.
verses.translation: Seeing their lord's condition, they wailed aloud. Their hair disheveled, they could not compose themselves. Beating their chests in many ways, they wept and recounted his glory.

verses.original:
तव बल नाथ डोल नित धरनी। तेज हीन पावक ससि तरनी।।
सेष कमठ सहि सकहिं न भारा। सो तनु भूमि परेउ भरि छारा।।
verses.transliteration: Tava bala nātha ḍola nita dharanī, teja hīna pāvaka sasi taranī. Seṣa kamaṭha sahi sakahi na bhārā, so tanu bhūmi pareu bhari chārā.
verses.translation: 'O lord, by your power the earth always trembled. Sun, moon, and fire lost their radiance. Shesha and the cosmic tortoise could not bear your weight — yet that body now lies covered in dust.'

verses.original:
बरुन कुबेर सुरेस समीरा। रन सन्मुख धरि काहुँ न धीरा।।
भुजबल जितेहु काल जम साईं। आजु परेहु अनाथ की नाईं।।
verses.transliteration: Baruna kubera suresa samīrā, rana sanmukha dhari kāhum̐ na dhīrā. Bhujabala jitehu kāla jama sāīṁ, āju parehu anātha kī nāīṁ.
verses.translation: 'Varuna, Kubera, Indra, Vayu — none could hold their ground before you in battle. By the might of your arms you conquered even Death. Yet today you lie fallen like one with no protector.'

verses.original:
जगत बिदित तुम्हारी प्रभुताई। सुत परिजन बल बरनि न जाई।।
राम बिमुख अस हाल तुम्हारा। रहा न कोउ कुल रोवनिहारा।।
verses.transliteration: Jagata bidita tumhārī prabhutāī, suta parijana bala barani na jāī. Rāma bimukha asa hāla tumhārā, rahā na kou kula rovanihārā.
verses.translation: 'Your lordship was known throughout the world. The strength of your sons and kinsmen was beyond description. O one who turned away from Rama — this is your state! No one remains even to mourn your clan.'

verses.original:
तव बस बिधि प्रपंच सब नाथा। सभय दिसिप नित नावहिं माथा।।
अब तव सिर भुज जंबुक खाहीं। राम बिमुख यह अनुचित नाहीं।।
verses.transliteration: Tava basa bidhi prapaṁca saba nāthā, sabhaya disipa nita nāvahi māthā. Aba tava sira bhuja jambuka khāhīṁ, rāma bimukha yaha anucita nāhīṁ.
verses.translation: 'Under your power, Lord, was all of creation. The frightened guardians of the directions always bowed their heads. Now jackals devour your heads and arms. For one who turns away from Rama, this is not improper.'

verses.original: काल बिबस पति कहा न माना। अग जग नाथु मनुज करि जाना।।
verses.transliteration: Kāla bibasa pati kahā na mānā, aga jaga nāthu manuja kari jānā.
verses.translation: 'Overtaken by fate, you did not heed my counsel. You took the Lord of the animate and inanimate to be a mere mortal.'

verses.original:
जान्यो मनुज करि दनुज कानन दहन पावक हरि स्वयं।
जेहि नमत सिव ब्रह्मादि सुर पिय भजेहु नहिं करुनामयं।।
आजन्म ते परद्रोह रत पापौघमय तव तनु अयं।
तुम्हहू दियो निज धाम राम नमामि ब्रह्म निरामयं।।
verses.transliteration: Jānyo manuja kari danuja kānana dahana pāvaka hari svayam̐. Jehi namata siva brahmādi sura piya bhajehu nahiṁ karunāmayam̐. Ājanma te paradroha rata pāpaughamaya tava tanu ayam̐. Tumhahū diyo nija dhāma rāma namāmi brahma nirāmayam̐.
verses.translation: 'You took the demon-forest-burning fire, Hari himself, to be a mortal. You did not worship the compassionate one, before whom Shiva and Brahma bow. From birth you delighted in harming others — your body was a heap of sin. Yet even to you Rama has given his own abode. I bow to Brahma, the blemishless, Rama!'

verses.original:
अहह नाथ रघुनाथ सम कृपासिंधु नहिं आन।
जोगि बृंद दुर्लभ गति तोहि दीन्हि भगवान।।104।।
verses.transliteration: Ahaha nātha raghunātha sama kṛpāsindhu nahi āna. Jogi bṛnda durlabha gati tohi dīnhi bhagavāna.
verses.translation: 'Ah, Lord! There is no ocean of mercy like Raghunath. The state that is difficult even for great yogis to attain — that has been given to you by the Lord!'

verses.original:
मंदोदरी बचन सुनि काना। सुर मुनि सिद्ध सबन्हि सुख माना।।
अज महेस नारद सनकादी। जे मुनिबर परमारथबादी।।
verses.transliteration: Mandodarī bacana suni kānā, sura muni siddha sabanha sukha mānā. Aja maheśa nārada sanakādī, je munibara paramārthabādī.
verses.translation: Hearing Mandodari's words, gods, sages, and siddhas were all gratified. Brahma, Shiva, Narada, the Sanakadi sages, and all the great sages devoted to the supreme truth —

verses.original:
भरि लोचन रघुपतिहि निहारी। प्रेम मगन सब भए सुखारी।।
रुदन करत देखीं सब नारी। गयउ बिभीषनु मन दुख भारी।।
verses.transliteration: Bhari locana raghupatihi nihārī, prema magana saba bhae sukhārī. Rudana karata dekhīṁ saba nārī, gayau bibhīṣanu mana dukha bhārī.
verses.translation: — all gazed upon Raghupati with eyes full of love and were immersed in bliss and delight. Vibhishana saw all the women weeping; with heavy grief in his heart, he went to them.

verses.original:
बंधु दसा बिलोकि दुख कीन्हा। तब प्रभु अनुजहि आयसु दीन्हा।।
लछिमन तेहि बहु बिधि समुझायो। बहुरि बिभीषन प्रभु पहिं आयो।।
verses.transliteration: Bandhu dasā biloki dukha kīnhā, taba prabhu anujahi āyasu dīnhā. Lachimana tehi bahu bidhi samujhāyo, bahuri bibhīṣana prabhu pahi āyo.
verses.translation: Seeing his brother's state, he grieved. Then the Lord instructed Lakshmana. Lakshmana consoled Vibhishana in many ways, and then Vibhishana returned to the Lord.

verses.original:
कृपादृष्टि प्रभु ताहि बिलोका। करहु क्रिया परिहरि सब सोका।।
कीन्हि क्रिया प्रभु आयसु मानी। बिधिवत देस काल जियँ जानी।।
verses.transliteration: Kṛpādṛṣṭi prabhu tāhi bilokā, karahu kriyā parihari saba sokā. Kīnhī kriyā prabhu āyasu mānī, bidhivata desa kāla jiyam̐ jānī.
verses.translation: The Lord looked upon him with a compassionate glance: 'Perform the last rites and set aside all grief.' Following the Lord's command, Vibhishana performed the rites properly, mindful of the place and time.

verses.original:
मंदोदरी आदि सब देइ तिलांजलि ताहि।
भवन गई रघुपति गुन गन बरनत मन माहि।।105।।
verses.transliteration: Mandodarī ādi saba dei tilāñjali tāhi. Bhavana gaī raghupati guna gana baranata mana māhi.
verses.translation: Mandodari and all the others offered their final water-oblations to him. They then went home, praising Raghupati's virtues in their hearts.

verses.original:
आइ बिभीषन पुनि सिरु नायो। कृपासिंधु तब अनुज बोलायो।।
तुम्ह कपीस अंगद नल नीला। जामवंत मारुति नयसीला।।
verses.transliteration: Āi bibhīṣana puni siru nāyo, kṛpāsindhu taba anuja bolāyo. Tumha kapīsa aṅgada nala nīlā, jāmavanta māruti nayasīlā.
verses.translation: Vibhishana came and bowed his head again. The ocean of mercy then called his brother Lakshmana: 'You, Sugriva the monkey king, Angad, Nala, Nila, Jambavan, and noble Hanuman —'

verses.original:
सब मिलि जाहु बिभीषन साथा। सारेहु तिलक कहेउ रघुनाथा।।
पिता बचन मैं नगर न आवउँ। आपु सरिस कपि अनुज पठावउँ।।
verses.transliteration: Saba mili jāhu bibhīṣana sāthā, sārehu tilaka kaheu raghunāthā. Pitā bacana maiṁ nagara na āvaum̐, āpu sarisa kapi anuja paṭhāvaum̐.
verses.translation: '— all go with Vibhishana and perform his coronation,' commanded Raghunath. 'Since my father's vow prevents me from entering a city, I send monkey warriors as my own representatives.'

verses.original:
तुरत चले कपि सुनि प्रभु बचना। कीन्ही जाइ तिलक की रचना।।
सादर सिंहासन बैठारी। तिलक सारि अस्तुति अनुसारी।।
verses.transliteration: Turata cale kapi suni prabhu bacanā, kīnhī jāi tilaka kī racanā. Sādara siṁhāsana baiṭhārī, tilaka sāri astuti anusārī.
verses.translation: Hearing the Lord's words, the monkeys departed at once. They went and arranged the coronation. They seated Vibhishana reverently on the throne and performed the anointing ceremony, followed by hymns of praise.

verses.original:
जोरि पानि सबहीं सिर नाए। सहित बिभीषन प्रभु पहिं आए।।
तब रघुबीर बोलि कपि लीन्हे। कहि प्रिय बचन सुखी सब कीन्हे।।
verses.transliteration: Jori pāni sabahīṁ sira nāe, sahita bibhīṣana prabhu pahi āe. Taba raghubīra boli kapi līnhe, kahi priya bacana sukhī saba kīnhe.
verses.translation: All folded their hands and bowed their heads. Then they came to the Lord together with Vibhishana. Raghubir called the monkeys and spoke sweet words, making them all happy.

verses.original:
किए सुखी कहि बानी सुधा सम बल तुम्हारें रिपु हयो।
पायो बिभीषन राज तिहुँ पुर जसु तुम्हारो नित नयो।।
मोहि सहित सुभ कीरति तुम्हारी परम प्रीति जो गाइहैं।
संसार सिंधु अपार पार प्रयास बिनु नर पाइहैं।।
verses.transliteration: Kie sukhī kahi bānī sudhā sama bala tumhārēṁ ripu hayo. Pāyo bibhīṣana rāja tihum̐ pura jasu tumhāro nita nayo. Mohi sahita subha kīrati tumhārī parama prīti jo gāihai. Sansāra sindhu apāra pāra prayāsa binu nara pāihai.
verses.translation: 'You are made happy by words as sweet as nectar. By your strength the enemy was slain. Vibhishana has obtained the kingdom; your fame shall remain ever new in the three worlds. Those who sing this glorious story of yours with great devotion, along with mine, shall cross the boundless ocean of worldly existence without any effort.'

verses.original:
प्रभु के बचन श्रवन सुनि नहिं अघाहिं कपि पुंज।
बार बार सिर नावहिं गहहिं सकल पद कंज।।106।।
verses.transliteration: Prabhu ke bacana śravana suni nahi aghāhi kapi puñja. Bāra bāra sira nāvahi gahahi sakala pada kañja.
verses.translation: Hearing the Lord's words, the host of monkeys could never be satisfied. Again and again they bowed their heads and clasped his lotus feet.

verses.original:
पुनि प्रभु बोलि लियउ हनुमाना। लंका जाहु कहेउ भगवाना।।
समाचार जानकिहि सुनावहु। तासु कुसल लै तुम्ह चलि आवहु।।
verses.transliteration: Puni prabhu boli liyau hanumānā, laṅkā jāhu kaheu bhagavānā. Samācāra jānakihi sunāvahu, tāsu kusala lai tumha cali āvahu.
verses.translation: Then the Lord called Hanuman and said, 'Go to Lanka. Give Janaki the good news and bring word of her well-being.'

verses.original:
तब हनुमंत नगर महुँ आए। सुनि निसिचरी निसाचर धाए।।
बहु प्रकार तिन्ह पूजा कीन्ही। जनकसुता देखाइ पुनि दीन्ही।।
verses.transliteration: Taba hanumanta nagara mahum̐ āe, suni nisicarī nisācara dhāe. Bahu prakāra tinha pūjā kīnhī, janakasutā dekhāi puni dīnhī.
verses.translation: Then Hanuman came into the city. Hearing the news, demonesses and demons rushed to him. They worshipped him in many ways and then showed him to where Sita was.

verses.original:
दूरहि ते प्रनाम कपि कीन्हा। रघुपति दूत जानकीं चीन्हा।।
कहहु तात प्रभु कृपानिकेता। कुसल अनुज कपि सेन समेता।।
verses.transliteration: Dūrahi te pranāma kapi kīnhā, raghupati dūta jānakīṁ cīnhā. Kahahu tāta prabhu kṛpāniketā, kusala anuja kapi sena sametā.
verses.translation: From a distance, the monkey bowed respectfully. Janaki recognized him as Raghupati's messenger. 'Tell me, dear one, is the merciful Lord well, along with his brother and the monkey army?'

verses.original:
सब बिधि कुसल कोसलाधीसा। मातु समर जीत्यो दससीसा।।
अबिचल राजु बिभीषन पायो। सुनि कपि बचन हरष उर छायो।।
verses.transliteration: Saba bidhi kusala kosalādhīsā, mātu samara jītyo dasasīsā. Abicala rāju bibhīṣana pāyo, suni kapi bacana haraṣa ura chāyo.
verses.translation: 'The Lord of Kosala is perfectly well in every way, Mother. He has defeated the ten-headed one in battle. Vibhishana has received the eternal kingdom.' Hearing the monkey's words, joy filled her heart.

verses.original:
अति हरष मन तन पुलक लोचन सजल कह पुनि पुनि रमा।
का देउँ तोहि त्रेलोक महुँ कपि किमपि नहिं बानी समा।।
सुनु मातु मैं पायो अखिल जग राजु आजु न संसयं।
रन जीति रिपुदल बंधु जुत पस्यामि राममनामयं।।
verses.transliteration: Ati haraṣa mana tana pulaka locana sajala kaha puni puni ramā. Kā deum̐ tohi trailoka mahum̐ kapi kimapi nahiṁ bānī samā. Sunu mātu maiṁ pāyo akhila jaga rāju āju na sansayam̐. Rana jīti ripudala bandhu juta pasyāmi rāmamanāmayam̐.
verses.translation: With great joy in heart and body thrilling with ecstasy, eyes brimming with tears, Rama's consort (Lakshmi) spoke again and again: 'What can I give you? In all the three worlds, O monkey, nothing is equal to this gift of words.' 'Listen, Mother, today without doubt I have obtained sovereignty over the entire world. Having conquered the enemy host in battle, with his brother, I shall behold Rama, free from all affliction.'

verses.original:
सुनु सुत सदगुन सकल तव हृदयँ बसहुँ हनुमंत।
सानुकूल कोसलपति रहहुँ समेत अनंत।।107।।
verses.transliteration: Sunu suta sadguna sakala tava hṛdayam̐ basahum̐ hanumanta. Sānukūla kosalapati rahahum̐ sameta ananta.
verses.translation: 'Listen, my son, may all good virtues dwell in your heart, O Hanuman! May the Lord of Kosala, with Ananta (Lakshmana), always remain gracious to you.'

verses.original:
अब सोइ जतन करहु तुम्ह ताता। देखौं नयन स्याम मृदु गाता।।
तब हनुमान राम पहिं जाई। जनकसुता कै कुसल सुनाई।।
verses.transliteration: Aba soi jatana karahu tumha tātā, dekhauṁ nayana syāma mṛdu gātā. Taba hanumāna rāma pahi jāī, janakasutā kai kusala sunāī.
verses.translation: 'Now, dear son, make arrangements so that I may behold his dark, gentle form with my own eyes.' Then Hanuman went to Rama and reported Janaki's well-being.

verses.original:
सुनि संदेसु भानुकुलभूषन। बोलि लिए जुबराज बिभीषन।।
मारुतसुत के संग सिधावहु। सादर जनकसुतहि लै आवहु।।
verses.transliteration: Suni sandesa bhānukulabhūṣana, boli lie jubarāja bibhīṣana. Mārutasuta ke saṅga sidhāvahu, sādara janakasutahi lai āvahu.
verses.translation: Hearing the message, the jewel of the solar dynasty summoned the crown prince Vibhishana: 'Go with Hanuman and reverently bring Janaki here.'

verses.original:
तुरतहिं सकल गए जहँ सीता। सेवहिं सब निसिचरीं बिनीता।।
बेगि बिभीषन तिन्हहि सिखायो। तिन्ह बहु बिधि मज्जन करवायो।।
verses.transliteration: Turatahi sakala gae jaham̐ sītā, sevahi saba nisicarīṁ binītā. Begi bibhīṣana tinahi sikhāyo, tinhi bahu bidhi majjana karavāyo.
verses.translation: They all went at once to where Sita was. Humble demonesses attended her. Vibhishana quickly instructed them, and they had Sita bathe elaborately.

verses.original:
बहु प्रकार भूषन पहिराए। सिबिका रुचिर साजि पुनि ल्याए।।
ता पर हरषि चढ़ी बैदेही। सुमिरि राम सुखधाम सनेही।।
verses.transliteration: Bahu prakāra bhūṣana pahirāe, sibikā rucira sāji puni lyāe. Tā para haraṣi caḍhī baidehī, sumiri rāma sukhadhāma snehī.
verses.translation: They adorned her with ornaments of many kinds and brought a beautiful palanquin. Vaidehi joyfully mounted it, remembering Rama, the beloved abode of bliss.

verses.original:
बेतपानि रच्छक चहुँ पासा। चले सकल मन परम हुलासा।।
देखन भालु कीस सब आए। रच्छक कोपि निवारन धाए।।
verses.transliteration: Betapāni racchaka cahum̐ pāsā, cale sakala mana parama hulāsā. Dekhana bhālu kīsa saba āe, racchaka kopi nivārana dhāe.
verses.translation: Guards with staffs surrounded her on all four sides. Everyone proceeded with great joy. All the bears and monkeys came to see her, but the guards angrily drove them away.

verses.original:
कह रघुबीर कहा मम मानहु। सीतहि सखा पयादें आनहु।।
देखहुँ कपि जननी की नाईं। बिहसि कहा रघुनाथ गोसाई।।
verses.transliteration: Kaha raghubīra kahā mama mānahu, sītahi sakhā payādēṁ ānahu. Dekhahum̐ kapi jananī kī nāīṁ, bihasi kahā raghunātha gosāī.
verses.translation: Raghubir said, 'Obey my command. Bring Sita here on foot, friend. Let the monkeys see her as they would their own mother.' The Lord of the Raghus smiled as he spoke.

verses.original:
सुनि प्रभु बचन भालु कपि हरषे। नभ ते सुरन्ह सुमन बहु बरषे।।
सीता प्रथम अनल महुँ राखी। प्रगट कीन्हि चह अंतर साखी।।
verses.transliteration: Suni prabhu bacana bhālu kapi haraṣe, nabha te suranha sumana bahu baraṣe. Sītā prathama anala mahum̐ rākhī, pragaṭa kīnhi caha antara sākhī.
verses.translation: Hearing the Lord's words, the bears and monkeys rejoiced. The gods rained many flowers from the sky. Sita had earlier been placed in the fire (her shadow-form); now the Lord wished to publicly establish the truth.

verses.original:
तेहि कारन करुनानिधि कहे कछुक दुर्बाद।
सुनत जातुधानीं सब लागीं करै बिषाद।।108।।
verses.transliteration: Tehi kārana karunānidhi kahe kachuka durbāda. Sunata jātudhānīṁ saba lāgīṁ karai biṣāda.
verses.translation: For that reason, the ocean of mercy spoke a few harsh words. Hearing them, all the demonesses began to grieve.

verses.original:
प्रभु के बचन सीस धरि सीता। बोली मन क्रम बचन पुनीता।।
लछिमन होहु धरम के नेगी। पावक प्रगट करहु तुम्ह बेगी।।
verses.transliteration: Prabhu ke bacana sīsa dhari sītā, bolī mana krama bacana punītā. Lachimana hohu dharama ke negī, pāvaka pragaṭa karahu tumha begī.
verses.translation: Placing the Lord's words on her head, Sita — pure in thought, word, and deed — spoke: 'Lakshmana, be the witness of this sacred rite. Prepare a blazing fire at once.'

verses.original:
सुनि लछिमन सीता कै बानी। बिरह बिबेक धरम निति सानी।।
लोचन सजल जोरि कर दोऊ। प्रभु सन कछु कहि सकत न ओऊ।।
verses.transliteration: Suni lachimana sītā kai bānī, biraha bibeka dharama niti sānī. Locana sajala jori kara doū, prabhu sana kachu kahi sakata na oū.
verses.translation: Hearing Sita's words, steeped in love, wisdom, duty, and right conduct, Lakshmana stood with tearful eyes and folded hands; he could not say a word to the Lord.

verses.original:
देखि राम रुख लछिमन धाए। पावक प्रगटि काठ बहु लाए।।
पावक प्रबल देखि बैदेही। हृदयँ हरष नहिं भय कछु तेही।।
